string.text: I need medical attention.
Let me take a look...
[continue...]
Alright, that’s it… You were worried for nothing, only your skirt got wrinkled. You can go.
Let's see… With health like that? Well, I guess I need a ticket to the morgue...
I need to fill my canteen with drinking water. Can you help?
No problem, my friend. Choose how much murky water you need.
Fill it one-third. (100 rubles).
Only one-third? Are you out of your mind, buddy? Why so little? That’s barely enough for anything...
That’s enough for now. Thanks.
Fill it halfway. (200 rubles).
Here you go. Why so little? You don’t respect Vazgen at all, huh? Could’ve filled it completely. Julio here – just half. Oh, what kind of people these days…
That’s fine for now. Thanks.
Fill it completely. (300 rubles).
Here you go. Wow, you respected old Vazgen. Vazgen is feeling generous today. Can’t pour the chacha into another bottle. They brought me some amazing chacha – wow, it hits hard, just awesome...
Sure, but later. Keep the chacha for now.
Changed my mind. I’ll come back later, for now I’m fine.
Damn. Don’t even have an extra hundred to buy drinking water.
So, idiot, hand over the cash or we’ll shoot you…
Cash? Why would a dead man need cash? He doesn’t, plus the gear and weapon too...
Hey you, jerk, don’t play smart. Pedal horse, empty your pockets. Last Chinese thing for you...
Let’s see how tough you really are...
Got the hint. I don’t need trouble. Take what’s here and let’s split.
Looks like you’ve got brains. Good. Don’t hang around too long. Better leave, these guys are edgy and might shoot…
Got it, I’m leaving now...
Go to hell, son of a bitch. Easier for me to just shoot you.
What the hell? You’ve gone crazy, I see. Don’t worry, my guys and I will teach you some manners.
Go ahead and try...
Alright. Tell me, what do you know about the "Clear Sky" faction?

Not much to tell. We’ve seen them around a couple of times. Once even a squad passed by. Though maybe it’s just rumors. What’s certain is they recently found a mutilated body in their uniform. Most likely killed by some local creatures.
The body is clear, but are you sure a whole squad was seen here?
A whole squad, you say. I doubt that. The body is clear, but the squad…
No way, like I said, probably just rumors. Maybe not. I don’t know, stalker.
Alright, thanks for that.
Listen, I wanted to ask you about the Clear Sky faction. Have you heard anything interesting about them?

I’ve heard a bit. Rumor has it one of their agents was killed here. Don’t know who or how, but that’s what people say. Naturally, the Freedomers didn’t poke their beaks in – an agent is an agent, even in Africa. Our guys could have easily let it slide. But it seems to me that it wasn’t our people who took him out. Maybe mercenaries, bandits, or someone else.
I see. This info helps connect some other facts. By the way, was that agent’s body found near the farm?
Now that changes things a lot. The pieces of the puzzle are coming together. By the way, was that agent’s body found near the farm?

Yeah, somewhere around there. And the strangest thing – our scouts found the body first and left it. Thought “whatever, he’s dead, that’s that.” So many stalkers die in the Zone. Later, they tried to bury it properly, but the body was gone. Vanished into thin air. Thought mutants dragged it off, but no body, no scraps of gear. Nothing. So now, who took him? That’s the question.
I don’t know who took it, but I have an idea where it resurfaced... Thanks for the info.
Now let’s talk about some information. Tell me, what do you know about the Clear Sky stalkers? I heard they were seen around here not long ago.

Locals talk a lot. Recently, they said they found a clearing in the forest with bodies of Freedomers and Duty members. Doesn’t seem like they killed each other. Either all at once, or some first, then someone finished off the survivors. As for the Clear Sky, I can’t tell you much. There were also sightings of strange stalkers, looked like scientists, but who knows. Nowadays anyone with money can buy a lab suit.
Wait. Forget about the Freedomers and others. Tell me more about the scientists – where, when, and how many?
Hold on, forget Clear Sky for now. About the scientists – I need details. When were they seen, where exactly, and how many?

Heh. You’re sharper than you seemed. Alright, since I promised info – here it is. About a week ago, in a dense part of the forest. A group of these “scientists” came from the east. About eight or nine, no one knows for sure. Scouts tried to track them the next day, but found nothing. Strange. Some say they were phantoms, others say no one was there. Personally, I think they just split up. Hard to track a group when everyone goes their own way. Our scouts are good, but not super-professional. That’s all I know, stalker. Believe me or not, your choice.
Alright. Thanks for your help.
Thanks. Your information helped me a lot.
About Clear Sky. I want to know how many stalkers were seen and when. Was it a squad or just individuals?

What I can say. A couple of people came here recently. Hung out at the bar, then left. Bought food and some ammo. Seemed like regular artifact hunters, nothing more. Standard stalker gear and weapons. Didn’t look like a combat group.
Did they buy food and ammo just for themselves, or for a bigger group?
And nothing else? Just food and ammo for themselves?
I can’t get into that much detail. Ask Vazgen yourself… They looked like they only bought for themselves. Scouts saw them leave our territory toward Yantar.
Understood. Thanks for the help.
Got it. Thanks for the help, at least.
Hey, any rumors about Clear Sky showing up, or maybe scientist stalkers wandering around recently?
Not that I heard. Freedomers were around recently, which is strange – far from their territory. Duty shows up often, though. Birds, I don’t recall.
What about the scientists? Yantar’s nearby, maybe someone came through?
We know about those. But the scientists – surely someone?

No, as I said. If anyone came, I’d remember. No info on the radios either. If anyone went missing or needed help – I’d know. Ask locals, maybe someone heard something. I don’t trust rumors, only what I hear on the radio.
Got it. Thanks.
Help me with one thing. Have any scientist stalkers appeared at your base, or has anyone seen them nearby?
Yeah, a while back, about three months ago. They sometimes come from Yantar. But last couple of weeks, no one.
Three months is a long time. So none in the last two weeks…
Three months is too long. Maybe someone came later…

Wait, I remember. Recently a stalker brought a torn, bloody lab suit. Obviously from a corpse. Asked me to fix it. Told him too damaged, impossible to restore. He left empty-handed. I bought it for parts for a thousand. Looks like the owner fell into some anomaly. That’s all I recall.
Got it. Thanks, that really helps.
Vazgen, help me out. Heard anything about Clear Sky or scientist stalkers? Seen them around here about a week ago?
Sure, no problem. Seen them more than once. Don’t know how this info helps you. I know people like you – everyone comes here for something. And it’s not just artifacts and money.
That’s not for you to decide. Just tell me what you know.
Just tell me what you know. I’ll decide if it helps me.
They were seen here. That’s all I can say for now. Look for more specific leads and you’ll find the clues.
Understood. Thanks.
Alright, let’s be specific. What can you tell me about my case?

You’ve caused quite a stir here. Your contract is your business. But to me, it looks suspicious. Dirty work, Varg. Very. Since I promised help, I will. You’ve collected facts about Clear Sky and scientists. Pass them to the contact.
When should I expect the contact?
When should I expect the agent?

Soon, my friend. Soon. Here’s what we know: Clear Sky squad here about a week ago. Our people saw a couple of them. One body found – likely their agent. The squad probably wiped out by mutants. Scientists seen around the same time. One body near an anomaly. Others unknown. Likely a hidden hideout in the forest. That’s the summary, collected from stalkers and scouts.
Thanks for your help. I won’t forget this.
Alright, let’s be specific. What can you tell me about my case?
Your contract is your responsibility. But since you helped me, I’ll help you. You’ve collected facts about Clear Sky and scientists. Pass them to your contact. He’ll appear here soon.
I see. So you have direct contact with Clear Sky?
So you have direct contact with the Birds?
Clear Sky contact? So you originally had direct access to the Birds?

Yes. Here’s what we know: Clear Sky squad here about a week ago. A couple of them seen by our people. One body – likely their agent. Squad probably wiped out by mutants. Scientists seen at same time. One body near anomaly. Others unknown. Likely a hidden forest hideout. That’s all. Summary collected from stalkers and scouts.
Thanks, Hasan. You really helped me, and I don’t regret choosing your side.
Thanks. I hope I won’t regret choosing your side.
Greetings. Looking for some work. Need a hand with anything?
Well, there’s a small job. The main thing is your willingness to work. Though usually I take more trusted people — I rarely hire random newcomers from outside.
Uh, I don’t look like a random newcomer, do I? Or is your artifact business so serious you only take trusted hands? So, what’s the job exactly? Give me details.

All you freelance stalkers are the same. Girls at the bar sell their bodies, you sell your artifact-finding services. So tell me — what’s the difference? Enough talk. Everyone’s hungry, everyone wants profit… I need measurement results.
My name’s Varg and I’m no whore. Remember that, or I’ll shoot your hand. Measurements, you say? Will I need to set up any anomaly or artifact scanners?

No, you won’t be setting them up. They were installed long before you arrived. Some even paid with their lives. Your task is to collect them and bring them here so I can get the measurement results. That’ll help coordinate our search groups better, and they can work on safer, more profitable routes. The Zone’s not calm these days. Artifacts in usual spots are scarce. You gotta find the juicy bits. No walk, no catch...
What kind of scanners? Can you elaborate? They measure anomalous activity — so they’re near major anomalies?
Just ordinary anomaly scanners. Their readings are needed by our techs to calibrate equipment. And for me, to prepare supplies and distribute everything among the groups.
Got it. Better suit then. Send me the coordinates.
Coordinates loaded. Suit is a good idea — take it. And don’t get cooked in anomalies.
Who installed them, and where do I have to go?
Does it matter who placed them? Hassan’s people did it a few days ago — satisfied? Don’t think about that. Your job is to run to the marked points, grab the scanners, and return alive. Clear?
Got it. Send coordinates.
Coordinates loaded. Wear a better suit. And watch out in anomalies.
Anything else I need?
No, though later I might find a more serious job for you. First, you’ll need the measurement results from the installed anomaly scanners.
Hunting scanners in anomalies. Ha, fun prospect. Can’t survive long there without a good suit...
Got it. Lucky for you I’m generous today. Here, got it from the quartermaster for just such a case. Not bad, though worn. Coordinates loaded.
Did they at least clean the blood from it? Must be off some buddy’s corpse...
Don’t start whining… You’re squeamish? My suit’s hardly fresh either. And I don’t ask what happened to its previous owner.
Right not to ask. I’m off for the measurements. One more thing — more info, not really a request...
Info, you say. Got it. Knew from the start you weren’t just some opportunist. Alright, talk when you return. Be careful — it’d be a waste if you die out there.
Understood. Heading out. By the way, one more thing — more info...
Info, huh. Sure, why not. I guessed you didn’t just wander here for nothing. Info comes after the job. Be careful — wouldn’t want your effort wasted.
Alright, I’ll do what’s needed. Where to start?
Now that’s a proper business talk. Here’s the plan. First, find the anomaly scanners. Hassan’s people installed them. Coordinates sent. Later, maybe another job, can’t promise though.
Got it. I’ll get your scanners. About another job — don’t count on it. I’m more interested in some info, which you might have...
Info, you say… why not. I suspected you didn’t come here for nothing. Talk when you return. Be careful. Don’t disappear in the anomalies.
Here are all your scanners. Honestly, didn’t expect you to handle this job. Usually it’s for techs or similar…

Strange question — I oversee artifact collection groups. Naturally, I ensure they work better. Smart people only handle what matters, friend. I’m smart. I do what our techs do, and more… The info you’re after is probably confidential. You get the hint… Good job, stalker. I’ll process the measurements. New work comes later, maybe not at all… Meanwhile, ask anything you want.
I’m ready to keep working. What needs to be done now?
Here’s the plan. We need to set up some backup caches for our clan fighters. It’s important work — the clan needs new stashes. And it’s time to set a little bait for our enemies as well.
By the way, did you manage to track down the previous looters? I’m interested in the specific culprits. Got a few ideas myself, and it’s good to know who you might run into.

By the way, did you manage to find the previous raiders? I want to know who exactly was involved. Good to know who you might face — if it’s just local thugs, that’s one thing, but if it’s pros, it changes everything.

Of course. It was one of the local punks. He pulled a little stunt, raiding one of our important caches. There was a lot of weaponry. We tracked the bastard to the nearest anomaly and, before taking him out, managed to learn that he raided the stash following someone’s tip. Hopefully now we’ll find out whose tip it was.

Bandits — that’s good in a way. They’re useless fighters. But here’s the thing. Info about these caches is only available to a select few for a short time, right? Eventually, the whole group will know, so what then?

Not quite. Though you’re close. You’re right that eventually everyone will know about the new caches. But for now, only the small circle you were told about has this info. No other leaks are possible. And my gut says the rat will show itself. And rest assured, silence and quality work will be generously rewarded.
Since it’s a delicate matter, I expect delicate payment. Some info, for example.
Delicate work deserves delicate payment. I hope you know what I mean?
Alright, I get it. I’ll do everything needed. But the payment will also need to be delicate…
We can work something out. For now, plant all the caches and come back here. Coordinates are already loaded for you.
Understood.
All caches are planted at the spots you indicated. What’s next? I think your plan worked.

Next… you’ll need to fill them with essential supplies: first aid, some food, a few packs of ammo. The full list is already loaded for you. Some expensive items you’ll get right now. Wait — you said my plan worked? Explain.
A group of thugs tried to rob me near the southern checkpoint. They were right by the spot I told the collector about. The one I mentioned to… Mahir.
A group of bandits tried to attack me near the spot I told Mahir about.
See. That’s Mahir. Good, my friend. At least now we have a lead. He knew you’d be in that area and could direct the thugs to you.
So now we know who sent them. That’s what you needed — the whole cache setup was designed to find out who would attack me. Since you were the bait, let’s discuss payment and guarantees.
Now you know who could have directed them to this area. Since you were used as bait in this clever trap, let’s sort out payment and guarantees.

Don’t worry. Guarantees there will be. You’re alive, so all is well. Smart kid, I see — you caught the triple trick immediately. That’s good. Fewer questions later. Now go finish the job I hired you for, then we’ll talk about your matter.
Right. I’ll ask questions only on my own topic. And I hope your answers won’t leave me disappointed…
All done. The caches are planted.
Good. Two birds with one stone. Caches help the stalkers, and I get my gain.

True. But I’m more interested in my third bird. You risked your life, after all… So I need info on any scientist stalker groups appearing here, as well as all Clear Sky members who visited in the past couple of weeks.
Birds are fine, but let’s get back to my matter. I need info on scientist groups appearing here, and all Clear Sky members who came in the last couple of weeks.

You risked your life for a reason. So that’s it — you’re after Clear Sky. Alright, let’s talk. Ask precise questions. Also, I’ll need to sort out the Mahir situation. Since you took this from the start, you’ll finish it. Come by when you’re ready.
Understood.
Hey, brother. Listen, help me out. I’ve got a radio device lying around — a kind of direction finder. Maybe you’ll buy it?
Direction finder, you say. And how much are you asking?
Three grand. Plus, you don’t ask where I got it from.
I’ll give it for three K. Also, no unnecessary questions about its origin.
Fair enough. Here you go. I wouldn’t ask anyway. Our clan’s stalkers don’t have such equipment, and the rest doesn’t interest me much. Even if you killed its previous owner.
Thanks for not refusing.
By the way, do you have any work available?
Work… tricky question. Depends on what you can or can’t do, and why exactly you need the work.
Got it. I’ll come by later, can’t work for you just yet.
Well, I need work to earn. Whether I can do something depends on what you can offer. I’m a broad specialist, and I’ve been in the Zone for years. Worked a long time under Orest from Agroyantar.

Broad specialist, huh? Alright, we’ll see what you’re good for. Being from Orest’s clan tells me little. What matters is how well you handle a soldering iron, not hunting artifacts or killing mutants. The job I have for you is specific. If you handle it — good.
I’m listening. What exactly do you need me to do?
Let’s get to the point. What’s required from me?
Can we get straight to it?

Briefly, here’s the thing. I’m in charge of radio communication and all related devices. I work closely with the technician and supply guy — but that’s another story. Here’s the matter: our clan has hidden radio stations here and on other locations. Don’t ask why. I think you already know. One of them seems down now. Unknown what happened. Your job — find out. Take some tools from me and get to it.
Alright. I’ll handle it.
So, I checked the radio station. Things look pretty bad.
What happened? Probably a couple of boards fried during the emission? Happens more than once.

Not exactly. Here, your multimeter back. One board is fried, yes. But looks like someone did it on purpose. Not random bandits — they’d tear it apart. Other clans probably wouldn’t do it either. Seems like a pro worked on it and knew what and where to break.

On purpose, huh? Interesting. Who’d need to sabotage this radio? Well, I’ll inform Hasan. Let them handle the saboteur. Our job is to restore it ASAP. Take the soldering station, fix it, and move it to the point I sent. Place it somewhere discreet. Put the station in a special case so it doesn’t stand out.
Alright. What about the settings? Its coverage area will change, right?
And what about the settings? Won’t coverage be different?
Looks like you know your stuff. Good questions. Don’t worry about the settings. I’ll handle it later. The area is already covered via backup channels.
Got it. Understood.
Done. The radio station is repaired and installed in a new location. You can proceed with setup and connecting the backup channel.

Excellent, my friend. Just return the tools you borrowed. You’ll get proper payment. You did well, stalker. Not everyone would tackle this job. Good work. You’ll go far if you don’t ask unnecessary questions.
Life’s full of questions we try in vain to answer. Here’s your soldering station. By the way, your leadership? How did they react to the sabotage news? Any ideas who did it?
Here’s your soldering station. By the way, how did your leadership react to the sabotage? Any idea who could’ve done it?

Who knows. They decided to be more vigilant. Though honestly, it’s useless while Harold and Hasan run things here. Strictly between us, they’re decent stalkers. But suspicious things keep happening. They just seek personal gain — passing artifacts to outside suppliers without anyone knowing. I wouldn’t be surprised if one of them ordered the equipment sabotaged to blame the other.

Wow. Interesting conclusions. At first, you didn’t seem so concerned with clan affairs. Aren’t you afraid to tell me this? And a leader versus his deputy — is one trying to take the other’s place? They already run everything… No, something else is at play. You mentioned suspicious things — any specifics?

Well, nothing unusual happened, really. Just the Zone things. A few artifact dropper groups disappeared, later found dead. Recently, the Healer was killed — our first medic, a clan founder. Body found in jelly. Most think he drunkenly got there, but he didn’t drink that night. Thinking about it, the picture is quite complex…
Well… fine. Not my concern much. I have my work. But it seems events here won’t pass me by. We’ll talk later about one guy — might be connected to what you described.
Hey, maybe you have some job for me? I’m new around here and looking for work. You’re a doctor, after all. Surely there’s something you need help with.

Honestly, I don’t have anything for you strictly related to my profession. But you can help with a personal matter — actually, two. Last time, I owe someone — need to return two artifacts to our dealer. Locals call him Vazgen. Central Asian guy… If you’ve ever dealt with this greed-driven bastard, you’ll know. I don’t like him, he doesn’t like me. Our characters… don’t mesh. Would have taken him out long ago, but the leadership and his, uh, “partners” would skin me for that. He can’t kick me out of the clan either… Central Asian tried, nasty piece, but failed. Recently the previous medic got eaten by the cold… there are no more doctors. That’s the story. The artifacts — that’s reality. Rare reality, like winning the lottery honestly — these aren’t lying around… And I’m a seeker like a frog is a ballerina. Maybe worse. So, if you find those artifacts — you’ll do me a solid. And yeah, buddy, a fancy detector will come in handy — I can hook you up. Free of charge. But I can offer another way to solve this. You’ll still have to do the same, but bigger risk. And pay will be higher. So, you in? Maybe later another task will pop up — more medical-related. Every day some greenhorn either flies into an anomaly or ends up on a boar’s tusks. Would be fine if they died, but no — they survive. So what do you do with these cripples afterwards?
Alright, I’ll help as much as I can.
No, I won’t work for you… at least not yet.
And what’s the second way to solve this problem?
Knew you’d be interested. Your job doesn’t change — still searching for artifacts. Just different ones, in another location. How I handle them next isn’t your concern.
Alright. That works for me. Now, tell me specifically what to find?

“Meat Chunk” and “Firefly.” Don’t look at me like that. Rare artifacts — I know. But I have little choice. Of two evils, pick the lesser. A friend, now gone, whispered one thing… A Griver appeared near the village. That Griver had those artifacts. The kid took care of the body, but the artifacts had to be hidden almost in the anomaly itself. Normal detectors used by our collectors can’t spot them. So, for now, they may still be there. If they are, grab them and bring them to me. Head to the thermal anomaly, I’ll figure out how to make the Central Asian take these, not other samples. Might even profit from it myself.
Got it. I’m heading out. I’ll try to find everything you mentioned.
Ah, I love dealing with a serious person.
And which artifacts are needed?
“Paradise Apple” and “Sparkle.” Pick your jaw up off the table. Never heard of them, huh? And you’re already ready to take the job? Something tells me I jumped the gun with you, huh?

Wow! Yeah, “rare”… If you told me to deliver a red Ferrari right into the Zone, it would surprise me less… Though I remember a businessman from Kyiv did exactly that once. Anyway, my choice is limited. The artifacts will come. I’m curious how you managed to land on these specific ones…

Well, don’t look at me. I won’t drag you by the ear. I’ll wait… If you find them, bring them here. Better to hand over such goods immediately and not linger, or you’ll end up on the missing list. Don’t ask about how you got this contract — cheaper to shoot you right here. So, move it. Say hi to your Grigovovich. He’s probably cruising the Zone in his Lamborghini…
Hold up, buddy — why so fast? These artifacts are rare and valuable, not in the nearest ditch. You might be a seeker, but you probably didn’t sit idle on your post either. Any leads?

Yeah, a bit. I checked with some knowledgeable folks… “Paradise Apple” — gravitational type, “Sparkle” — electric, both tricky. Artifacts here are a mess. They can appear almost anywhere. There is a system, but who will tell you, newbie? Life in the Zone isn’t easy without tricks. Search where there’s lots of radiation and gravitational clusters, or where “little lights” fly. Both are natural modifiers. As Jorah explained, they like not only their anomaly but others too. If our fat… sailor isn’t lying, “Apple” needs radiation, and “Shard” — chemistry stuff, like “Jelly” or “Burning Fur.” Got it? Best near the hamlet in radioactive piles. Go, and dive in.
Here’s to success. Farewell.
I found something here…
Whoa… kid, looks like you’re not messing around.
Here you go — “Sparkle.” Took some effort to find it…
Yep. Only “Apple” so far. Halfway there, huh?
Here’s both — “Apple” and “Sparkle.” If not for your lead, I’d have no chance. Take it, figure out the rest yourself.
Well done! Not a chance most stalkers would not only find but deliver such artifacts. Solid. I’ll deal with Vazgen later, sort out the debt… For now, let’s get back to your question, agreed?
No results yet. Will continue searching, will signal if anything comes up.
Good job, found “Sparkle”… Big deal. That’s only half the work. Once we get the “Apple,” we’ll tackle the other job.
Very good. “Apple” is perfect, but without the “Shard,” my debt to the Central Asian isn’t cleared. Move your hooves, the evening won’t wait. The profit is yours, not mine. Move it, young one.
Excellent, both artifacts delivered. Now our conversation can proceed properly.
Done, my friend. Artifacts are found. Here, take them.

Thanks. Now I can call you a friend. Keep the detector — such a powerful device will surely come in handy. Consider it part of the payment. And take your share in cash. Now we can talk on a different note. Maybe even offer you a slightly different job.
Thanks for that. That good detector will be useful. By the way, have you decided what to do about your debt? As I understand, you owed the dealer different artifacts. How will you handle that now?
We’ll sort it out. Like I said — this business is no longer your concern. Forget about the artifacts. Any other questions?
Alright, let’s move on.
So, enemy of Central Asia, what other job can you offer?

Listen, stalker. No jokes now. I just got word from the Limping Squad. One of their scouts is seriously wounded. We don’t know exactly what happened, maybe a psi-strike. Could be a controller. Go to the village as fast as you can.
And what should I do with this scout?
So what are my orders? What should I do with this scout?

What else — treat him, damn it. Listen, don’t push my buttons. I’d go myself, but Hasan, the second Central Asian, sent me on some urgent task. Long story short — someone’s dying, and he wants something done. You wanted work — here it is. Here’s a medkit with everything needed for first aid. Give it to the scout later.
Got it. Heading out.
Stalker, help… it’s in my head — I don’t know what it is… I can’t bear it anymore.
Wait — don’t move. Let’s take a look…
Thanks… What was that? My brain felt like it was burning alive…
Not sure what kind of mess you stepped into. Looks like a psi-strike. Did you see a controller before it happened or anyone similar? Maybe an unknown psi-anomaly? What do you remember?
There were no anomalies. I only remember one person. A human. Some stalker. He passed by and went into the forest. Definitely not a controller.
Got it. Thanks for the info. Bye.
Good. I’m here on Elimey’s orders. Your man was wounded…
Greetings, wanderer. Why didn’t the medical guy come himself? I told him urgent help was needed. Are you some kind of top healer? And what happened to my man?
Your man died. Too severe injuries… I couldn’t help him in time. Take the medkit — unused.
I see. So why did I even need such a doctor? Maybe I should just finish you off too. Elimey, damn cunning rat — hides at base and sends some newbie instead, who can’t even reach the place…

Calm down, scout. I’m on my own here. This work for Elimey is a one-time thing. He warned your man might not survive — that’s why he sent me. Hasan, your deputy, delayed him anyway… No point in me getting into trouble.

So… Elimey says Hasan delayed him. I see. Your fault then is none. Alright. What else did that bastard ask to deliver? You say you worked for the doctor once. So, you don’t mind mercenary work. Not from the blue gang, are you?
Nothing else. I have no ties with the blue gang. I care more about the feathered ones. And what exactly happened to your man? I need detailed info.
I have no ties with mercenaries. I’m interested in the Clear Sky folks. And what exactly happened to your man? I need as much detail as possible.

Feathered, you say… Clear Sky influence here is minimal. We can discuss in detail. About the dead stalker — strange stuff. Maybe he stumbled into an anomaly or got hit by a controller or psi wave. But what worries me is…
Let’s talk in more detail…

Your fighter survived. I managed to save him. He told some strange things. Elimey didn’t come — Hasan detained him. Take the medkit. Might be useful for someone else. Elimey instructed you to deliver it later.
Hasan, huh. Alright… What exactly did the wounded tell you?
He said he saw someone. I think it was a controller. His mind was under psi-attack. He might have seen something, or now remembers the illusion the mutant created in his head.

You think so, stalker? Maybe, but I’m not sure. Psi-types are often more dangerous than controllers, because you can’t tell them from humans. Likely it was him. We don’t need that mess here… Pray it was just a controller. And thanks for saving the kid’s life. If you want to work for our clan or just chat — I’m open.
Alright.

I’m new here. Don’t know much about your clan. As I understand, you’re the leader of the local scouts — not just any stalker. I’m mainly interested in work and the Clear Sky faction. Can you help me get started?

I can help. Just use the help wisely. Try to harm my clan — you die. Resist — you die slowly. Help — you’ll be well rewarded. I won’t discuss your feathered affairs, only info that won’t hurt the clan. About work — I have one risky job. Does that suit you?
Alright. Let’s talk about your task. What exactly do I need to do?
Sure. Let’s start with your task. What do I need to do?

I like your approach, stalker. Good. Serious task, serious risk. Pay matches. You need to scout an area. Near us is an old farm. Not a key area, but usable for a base, or at least a forward point. Artifact hunters’ caravans pass through. Hasan doesn’t like the idea of occupying it. But at least scout properly. Recently, mercenaries were spotted. Not sure why they were there. Go check if anyone remains and if they left traps. Here, take the mine detector — be careful.
Alright, clear on mine clearing. But if mercenaries are there? I can’t fight a whole brigade. Will you cover me somehow?

No brigade, just a few fighters. One of my men will be nearby, covering you. Don’t try to find him… He’ll reveal himself when ready. Farm is quiet, no traces of mercs. Even if someone is there, the two of you can handle it. Focus on clearing mines. Also, track signs of our blue friends. They won’t leave notes, but some traces will remain.
Understood. Will do.
The mercenary friends left us a few surprises. The mine detector came in handy. Any group of free stalkers who got here before me would have surely blown up.
Understood. Good work, stalker. By the way, did your cover man spot anything?
No. You trained your scouts well. You won’t see them in the forest. But they could take you out without you knowing how or from where. These guys are professionals.
Scouts are the eyes and ears of our faction. Alright, moving on — did you find anything suspicious besides a couple of planted mines?
Nothing special. Some traces of a recent campfire, leftover food. Looks like they used this spot as a temporary stop.

Alright, stalker. I hate to disappoint you, but your work seems wasted. Hasan, that jackal, canceled the request to occupy the abandoned farms. Something’s off, my friend. And you, apparently, aren’t entirely honest with me… Better not move. Two of my best men have you in their sights. Reach for a weapon — you’ll get a bullet between the eyes. Got the hint?
More than clear…
Understood. What do you want?

You say the found campsite was temporary? Then how do you explain that mercenaries were spotted here repeatedly? And how do you explain that after you checked the farm, another of my men found a dead mercenary there? Think carefully before answering…
I’ll answer your questions only if we survive this. Guns ready — a wave of creatures is coming…
By the way, maybe you have another job for me?

Let me think… Not that, not that… I think this one will suit you. You handled the broken radio well, so I think this is within your skill. We got orders from HQ — enhancing vigilance. Working with radars, in short.
Radars, got it. I’ll take the job. But where did you get them from?

After that case and some others, the leaders decided to set up a special monitoring system using radar stations. I have no idea where our usual latecomers managed to get them. Radar "Credo-1E" — rings a bell? Unique military equipment brought to the Zone. Now we have to deal with it.
Radars, huh? Not really in the mood for that right now. I’ll come later.
Nope, I’m not interested in messing with radar stations for now.
We’ll manage. Not my first time. What should I start with?
Alright, we’ll organize everything properly. Where do I start?

First, install the main radar modules at key points covering the main surveillance zones. The main module will be disguised as a simple wooden box, common in the Zone. I’ve configured the modules — battery, transceiver, primary processing unit, others. Each has configurable parameters. You fix them in place; I’ll connect remotely and check. If the signal works — fine. If not — we’ll troubleshoot.
Got it. Heading out.
I think I handled your task. Radars installed, modules in place. All done? Did you check their operation?
Checked. Two work fine. The third is problematic. Signal is off, like a settings glitch. Take the remote and go near that radar. Connect directly and adjust the input parameters.
You think this will help?
Are you sure this will work?
Any other ideas? Sure, you could disassemble a faulty unit, take it to the point, check modules against the working one… But that takes at least two days. So follow my instructions.
Alright.
So, I reprogrammed it. Should give correct readings now. Check it. Here’s your configuration device.
Hold on, let me quickly restart the system… There! Works perfectly. Excellent job.
And what will you do with this system now?
You set up a monitoring system like it’s a nuclear facility.
Not clear — why such an advanced monitoring system? Track swamp mutants or what?

Well… first, it’s not my whim. The boss said “do it” — I said “done”. We’re not poor or stingy. Huge money here — artifact hunting, mutant safaris… This is just a fraction. Harold makes money by the shovelful… And invests heavily in security. Equipment is like in private armies of Arab sheikhs. Cut corners — you’re dead. Show weakness — competitors crush you. And random bandits? Keep them in check too…
Got it. I understand. Doesn’t really concern me. I did my job, got my cut. Bye…

Phew, we held them off. Honestly, I didn’t expect that. Scout, don’t hold a grudge. You’re looking for enemies in the wrong place. I’m here for my own interests, and it’s not in my plans to harm your clan… Any questions? No problem, I’ll answer…

Held them off… you can say that. Of course I have questions. I see you truly don’t want to harm my faction, at least for now. If you still wanted to — I can’t prove it yet. If you were a mercenary, you’d have killed me as soon as we got rid of the creatures. Not certain, but still… Alright, stalker. Answer this: why didn’t you mention the mercenary’s corpse?

I told you — I have my own interest here, connected to the “Clear Sky” clan. Clues I find, I’ll use as I see fit. In that case, I have a counter-proposal… Help me investigate the disappearance of the Clear Sky group and the scientists in your area, and I’ll try to resolve your faction’s internal conflicts.

Heh, you’re a clever one, stalker. Handling internal conflicts isn’t easy, huh? Well, fine. I can share some information. My guys tracked the Clear Sky members — they were here about a week ago. Where they are now — I don’t know. They left our controlled area, and we lost interest. Regarding the scientists, some will say they saw them, some won’t. Here’s what I tell you — my people didn’t see them. And if my scouts didn’t see them — they weren’t here. Interpret that as you wish…
I see. Well, thank you for the information.
Now that’s interesting. Thanks for the information.
And one more thing. You’ll be surprised to learn that the mercenary corpse you found wasn’t actually a mercenary.

Should I be surprised? Honestly — I haven’t decided yet… What’s going on here? Internal conflicts in your faction? Why is your Hasan, as you call him, “that damn bastard”? What are the mercenaries doing here?

Heh, my friend — that’s exactly what I want to find out. Hasan is our deputy. He and Harold founded this clan together. Lately, their paths started diverging. We suspect Hasan may be running a double game, selling artifacts through shady channels, bypassing Harold. We can’t prove it yet. The mercenaries we’ve been observing likely work for him. Plus, he has reliable people inside the clan. Our faction is splitting into two. My advice — don’t get involved, stalker. You’d just become another pawn, used and forgotten.
Who knows. Maybe a pawn, maybe a queen. Either way, your internal conflict doesn’t concern me — at least until it crosses into my business.
Even pawns can checkmate the king. Either way, your internal conflict doesn’t concern me — at least until it affects my interests.

Remember — it’s in your interest that they don’t intersect. Though I suspect it’s inevitable. The corpse you found — one of the Clear Sky members, killed and disguised as a mercenary. I didn’t want to tell you. But you would’ve figured it out eventually. Since it’s neither a mercenary nor a stalker from our clan — you deal with it yourself.
Understood. Thanks. I’ll keep that in mind.
Alright, we’ll handle the main issues later. Need any more help from me? Nothing involving internal faction disputes. I’m fine with taking another job.

My group has two secondary tasks left. One involves measuring radiation at a newly emerged anomaly hotspot. The other is to measure activity of a chemical formation. If you want — you can take either.
I’ll take the radiation measurements.
Investigating the chemical anomaly suits me better.
Not interested in such work for now.
Good. Here’s a special radiometer. Handle it carefully — it’s rare and expensive here. Instructions are inside. Go to the designated area and complete the four measurements. Good luck.
On my way. Less risky than mine-clearing, thankfully.

Alright, then. Honestly, I hoped you’d take the radiation task — don’t want to risk extra doses ourselves. Here, take the good old VPHR. You know how to handle it. Take samples from one anomaly and return. Got it?
Understood. I’ll do it properly.
Task completed. Measurements done. Some spots have very high radiation. This anomaly should produce artifacts regularly.
Good work. Hand over the radiometer and the results. And again — take my advice. Things here are serious. Even too serious… Your investigation might take you too far, with no turning back.
Here’s the device and results. I know there might be no turning back. But I have no choice…
I have no choice. Either way, I’m already in this mess; I’ll try not to drown. Here are your results.
There’s always a choice, my friend. Remember that. If needed — you can count on my help. And beware of Hasan. Farewell…
I’ll keep that in mind. Farewell…
Task completed. Samples taken. Quite a strange anomaly, but it should produce artifacts reliably.
Good work. Hand over the VPHR and the samples. Still… take my advice. Things here are serious. Even too serious… Your investigation might take you too far… So far that there’ll be no turning back.
Here’s the device and results. I understand there may be no way back. But I have no choice…
I have no choice. Either way, I’m already in this mess; I’ll try not to drown. Here are your results.
There’s always a choice, my friend. Remember that. If needed — you can count on my help. And remember — beware of Hasan. Farewell.
I’ll keep that in mind. Farewell…
Hey, stalker… Hold up. No nonsense. We’ll talk and let you go. Who are you, which faction? And what brings you to these parts?
Good to see you too, friend. Freedom member? And what are the fathers of anarchy doing in these remote lands, right next to Duty territory?
Are you deaf? Or should I shoot your knee to make you hear? We ask the questions here. Answer if you want to live.
Calm down, calm down. They call me Varg. From Agroyantar. Not tied to Duty. Don’t worry. I was in Orest’s faction. Then I came here, to the free life.
On business here, or what?
Just looking for artifacts, maybe find some work… But you clearly aren’t local. What brings you here?
You’re too curious, stalker. We’re waiting for someone. But apparently, it’s not you.

Apparently? So you don’t even know who you’re waiting for? Heh, alright. By the way, tell me — where can I earn some money around here? Even if you’re not local, you’ve clearly been here longer than me.
You don’t know who you’re waiting for? Funny. By the way, do you know where one can find work? You’ve been around longer than me, though not local.

We can take you to our commander. Actually, that’s a good idea — maybe you’ll be useful to him if you don’t ask too many questions. Or you can play the role of the stalker we’re waiting for. Just a thought. I’ve already bored my guys standing here waiting. Just kidding, of course. If you want more serious work — you should head straight to the Free Stalkers’ base. They have everything — blackjack, girls. Everything, as it should be.
Thanks, friend. I’ll probably see your commander first — maybe he can help.
Good choice. He set up a camp nearby with his guys. Farewell, Varg…
Farewell.
Hey, Freedom. Passing by, thought I’d drop in…
Drop in, you say? Whose side are you on, stalker? What brings you here?
Easy there. Boss. I’m here on business. One important matter — if you know what I mean. Your patrol said you were here, so I came…

Patrol, huh. Got it. We were expecting you. Well, not everyone. The scout was with us. He told us to wait for you here — then left. It’s been three days since he’s been gone. Staying here is unsafe — not your home. Do the task and get out.
Two days you say? That’s bad. Any Duty members along the way?
Doesn’t seem like him to disappear that long. Duty? They haven’t tracked you, I hope?

No Duty along the way. Well, almost none. Two artifact collectors appeared here — we dealt with them and buried them under a tree. Don’t worry, no tails. So? Are you going to do what we’ve been waiting for, damn it?
Your scout is needed. That’s one. Second — plans changed a bit. What’s the news on the “Clear Sky” faction?
What news could there be? And what does that have to do with this? Our task is…

Your task, fool, is to listen and follow orders. I said the tasks have changed. I won’t discuss them with you — only with your scout. Since he’s not here — answer my questions. Have you seen any Clear Sky stalkers in this area?
Yes, we have. But again… what does that have to do with our task?
Not your task, but mine. Did you try to find the scout? Maybe he’s lying nearby, guts ripped out, dying two steps from the camp.
Not yours, but mine. Did you try to find the scout?
He ordered not to search for him. To wait for you. Though, if you want — you can find him yourself. He’s your man after all…
When did he last contact you?
Two days ago. Here, take the detector. I don’t like this… Find him yourself. This isn’t clean. Not according to plan. I won’t move from here with my people.
Alright, sit tight, grow your rear. When I return, you’ll tell me everything you know about Clear Sky.
Hey there, folks. What happened here? Throwing a party or what? What’s the fight about?
None of your business, dog. This is Duty’s matter. Better leave while you’re still whole… Wait. Who are you and how did you find this place?
Got it. I’m not a fool, I’m leaving. I’m a local, called Varg. I was searching for artifacts nearby, heard gunfire, thought I’d check it out. Maybe someone needs help…

Local, you say? Searching for artifacts, you say? Local artifact collectors don’t roam alone. That’s the clan rule. So, who’s the commandant in your clan? If you’re local — you should know their name and what happened to them…

Well, I wander around — not just an artifact collector, you know. I’m not obliged to report to the first stranger I meet. As for the commandant — how should I know? First day I got my gear and was told: go, serve the motherland.

You’re right about one thing. No need to explain to every passerby. But regarding the commandant — see, here’s the thing — there is no commandant in your local clan. How do you explain that? We just took out some Freedom members, and you appeared here, at this spot…
Damn, lady luck has run out after all…
Hey there, hunter. I heard you went alone after the controllers and came back alive? Is that true?
Maybe true, maybe not. Who knows what drunk folks babble. What’s it to you? Who are you anyway? Don’t recall seeing you around here.
I’m Varg. Stalker. Just a stalker. Recently arrived. Not in a clan, so you wouldn’t remember me.
I’m not local. Name’s Varg. Not in a clan. That’s why you don’t remember.
Varg, huh. So what do you need, Varg?
What else — money, women, honor, respect. Can anyone wish for anything else?
Everyone has desires. Not everyone wants what you mentioned. Money and women — plenty can get that with a gun. But honor and respect… Are you really so naive to think it’ll help you?
Why not? It’ll bring even more women and money.
Heh, I’m starting to like you, Varg. And I assume you came here seeking all that?
Well, more or less, yes.
Exactly, my friend.

Remember this. Here you’ll find only trouble for your ass. But fine. Since you came, whether on your own or tipped off — choose. You can either destroy a swamp mutant nest or clear the area of more dangerous mutants.
I’m more interested in the swamp mutants.
I’ll take the more dangerous ones.

Good. Glad you’re not as foolish as I thought. Here’s the deal. Artifact collectors ran into unknown creatures. All the guys died. Scouts didn’t see it coming. And that’s not all. A few others recently disappeared in that area, all at night. One was apparently from Clear Sky. So who knows if you did the right thing. Don’t worry — you can go there not alone if you wish.
Wait. You said a Clear Sky member? I’ll handle this alone. Don’t need help.

Heh, I see you’re not only after women and glory… Fine, your choice, stalker. About the Clear Sky member — we’ll talk after the task. About help — I’d advise not refusing. Extra firepower won’t hurt. Anyway, my squad will be nearby. You’re going to them now. Their leader, Finist, will tell you more about the creatures and where they were seen. Take better gear. Off you go.
Alright.

Boy, thought this through well? I think so. Won’t stop you — your choice. The creatures came here at night. Not at the base, but close. Three men missing by morning. Clear Sky members came recently too. One of theirs also disappeared the next night. Scouts tracked them, didn’t engage. Scouts rarely do. They gave approximate area of the creatures, which have some psi abilities. Be really careful.
Wait, you said Clear Sky?

Heh, see? You’re not only after women and fame… Fine, your right, stalker. About the Clear Sky member — we’ll talk after you finish the task, if you return. Others are heading to that area, so you’ll probably not be alone. Take better gear. Off you go.
Farewell.
You’re Finist? Zmeelov said to find you. You need to tell me more about the swamp mutant nest.
First — I owe no one anything. Heh, maybe my ex-wife. I don’t care what you say. I’ll do only what Zmeelov ordered.
And what did he order?
Exactly what he ordered?

He ordered to watch you. See if you can handle it. Said you wanted to do it all yourself. So me and my guys won’t interfere. Die if you die. But if you do it right — return, I’ll have a favor to ask. Oh, forgot — the nest is near an abandoned farmstead. Not far. Go and take out everything that moves.
Don’t worry, I’ll return…
The swamp creatures are eliminated.
Good job, stalker. What exactly were they?
Hell if I know… Some mutated half-lizards, half-fish. Never seen anything like them. And I doubt they’re exactly what we were looking for.

Unlikely. We’ve never seen fish-like lizards here. We’ll need to collect one of the bodies and send it to the scientists in Yantra. Also, you’re going back to Zmeelov anyway — give him this, I owe him.
Heh, didn’t you say you owe nothing to anyone?
I’ll deliver it. But you said you owe nothing, right?
I lied. Fine — that’s my business. Just deliver it. Goodbye.
Alright. Farewell.
Task completed. The creatures are dead. I didn’t need Finist’s help, just like I said.
Excellent, stalker, excellent. You’ll get a good reward. Enough for blackjack and women. Hmm… I’ll think of another job for you later. But we’ll see.
Oh, I almost forgot. Finist asked me to give you his debt. Six thousand. By the way, what about the job? And my question about the Clear Sky member?
Ah, right. Finist asked me to pass you his debt — three thousand. What’s the work you wanted to offer? And my question?
What other job can you offer me? Ah, remembered — Finist asked to tell you he’ll repay the debt after his run. By the way, what about the Clear Sky member?

Debt is good. But Finist doesn’t owe me anything. What are you talking about? Did we nod heads in sync? Never mind. About the Clear Sky member — I’ll tell you what I know. The job — later. I need to decide if I can trust you with it. Did you really see Finist and his people?
Yes, he was there. Talked to him just like now with you. So about the job later — fine. Though you could’ve given a hint of what to do.

Debt is good. But Finist doesn’t owe me anything. What are you talking about? Did we nod heads in sync? Never mind. About the Clear Sky member — I’ll tell you what I know. The job — later. I still need to decide if I can trust you. Did you really see Finist and his people?
Yes, he was there. Talked to him just like now. Job later — fine. Though you could’ve hinted at what to do.

Debt is good. But Finist doesn’t owe me anything. What are you talking about? Did we nod heads in sync? Never mind. About the Clear Sky member — I’ll tell you what I know. The job — later. I still need to decide if I can trust you. Did you really see Finist and his people?
Yes, he was there. Talked to him just like now. Job later — fine. Though you could’ve hinted at what to do.
Hey, stalker. What are you doing here? You killed those creatures? That was our job.
Doesn’t concern me… First come, first served.
Doesn’t concern you, huh? We’ll tear your guts out then, dumbass. Give me what Zmeelov promised to pay, or else.
Easy, easy. Here, take it. Can’t even joke.
Good. Now get lost. Mention us to Zmeelov and you’re dead.
Got it. Leaving.
Don’t you get me, freak? I said in plain Russian — I don’t care. My job — my pay.
So you’re cheeky. Fine — I’ll teach you some manners.
Let’s see who teaches whom…
Screw you… Bastard. You think I’ll pay for you doing your job?
Don’t understand Russian? Fine, I’ll explain differently.
Go ahead, try…
Anyway, it’s done. Those creatures are destroyed. They were really dangerous, but I’ve encountered them once in the Agro-Yantra underground. No big issues.
Good. You’ll get paid. Are you sure there were no problems? Nothing else to tell me?
Absolutely. Nothing more to tell. On the contrary, I hope you’ll tell me something about my topic.
No problems. Nothing more to say. I hope you’ll give me some info on my subject.

I will, don’t worry. Here’s the thing — one of my best groups disappeared in the area where you worked. No witnesses. I decided to shut down operations for a bit — all safaris close for entertainment. Did you notice anything unusual?
Yes, exactly. I told you, didn’t see anyone — so I didn’t see anyone.
Hi, friend. You a local mechanic? I’m Varg, a free stalker from Agro-Yantra. Looking for work before I settle. Got anything for a skilled and handy wanderer?
Hello. Skilled and experienced, you say? Good. Familiar with tech? Not a master of everything — just not clumsy. At least able to hold a screwdriver.
You flatter me, friend. Would I roam the Zone if I were clumsy? I’d have been buried in the nearest anomaly long ago.

Fine. Hope you’re honest. Doesn’t matter to me — someone else would do the job. But time is money. Or lives. The task: as a group technician, I need to prepare purchases for the clan — select equipment, supplies, etc. Currently deciding what gear to buy in bulk. Choose: test gas masks or test portable radios. Need results to decide which models to order.
I’ll test gas masks. More relevant to a regular stalker.
I’ll work with radios. I studied communications in the military, so that’s my field.

As you wish. Plan: take necessary equipment from the nearby backpack. Prepare five test samples. Test in the designated tent — like army gas mask tests. Then bring results to me. Make sure to finish all five in time, or no pay.
Don’t worry. I’ll manage in time.
Fine. Task: five radio samples. Go to the area, check signal reception. Bring results to me. Our radio operator will monitor it.
Alright, no problem.
Wow. Serious job. Maybe I’ll come back later.
Task completed. That turned out to be pretty simple.

Well, that was all soft and easy for you. Some people can’t even handle such a simple task. Good job, kid. If another job comes up, I’ll let you know. By the way, have you thought about joining our clan? We need capable guys like you. I can put in a good word for you with the leadership.
Thought about it at first. But I need to sort out my own business first. After that — who knows.
Had a similar idea. But I need to handle my own business first. We’ll see after that.

Your own business? Maybe I can help. If you need technical assistance, don’t hesitate. Whatever’s needed — we’ll do it properly. By the way, if you want more work — come by at night, after three. If you’re interested, of course.
Thanks. I’ll keep that in mind.
Gas masks tested. Got the results. Is that all? That’s the entire job?
Yes. Isn’t that enough for you?
Well, kind of. I was hoping for something bigger. Didn’t even have to leave the base much.
Yeah, always want more. And here, you barely had to leave the base.

Heh. You’re cheeky, stalker. Wanting more. Many would consider this work lucky — no risk to your own ass. If you like risk, join hunters, suppliers, or scouts. They’ll give you real adrenaline. I can only offer tasks like this. If more work interests you — come after three at night.
Alright, don’t grumble like an old man. Got it.
Listen, friend. Got myself in trouble. Didn’t manage to test all the samples in time.

What, what? You got in trouble? Clumsy deer. I knew it. What kind of people are coming to the Zone now — I’m shocked. Complete idiots. And how are you still alive? And why are you just standing there blinking? Ah, son of a… Give me the equipment. You shouldn’t be trusted with anything.

Here, take it. Well, mistakes happen. I’ll fix it. Not everything works perfectly on the first try. Skill with tech is needed… I spent the last six months just shooting mutants and crawling through anomalies.
You’re still here? Fine — whatever. Maybe you’re skilled at something else. But don’t expect any more work from me. I wouldn’t even trust you to carry a dirty bucket.
Alright. Just don’t grumble.
Fine. Just don’t complain.
Damn, Freedom fighter… Where’s the medic? Who are you and where did you come from? Help…
Relax. Hold still, I’ll give you an injection. Struggle — and you’ll die in minutes. Barely made it to you. There… now it’s fine. Feeling better?
Yes, better. Who are you anyway?

A stalker from the Freedom faction. That’s enough for now. Don’t worry — I don’t hold grudges over the skirmish with our groups and the debt groups. Otherwise, would I be saving you now? Didn’t you fail to crack a mercenary stash?
Yes. Wait, how did you know?
What? How did you find out?
I know a lot. You’re not the first to fall for their poisoned stash trick. They fill it with gas. No chance of surviving without special gear. But you survived. Thanks to me, of course.
Thank you. Name your reward — artifacts, money. I’ll give everything.
I’ll ask later. For now, go to the base and see the medic. Say you inhaled chemical anomaly gases. He’ll heal you. I’ll check in when needed. And no unnecessary questions for now.
Alright.
As you say.
Hey, I need your help. Got myself in a chemical anomaly. Inhaled all kinds of gases. Can you check me? Even with some meds, I still feel bad.

In an anomaly, you say? Let’s take a look. Are you joking? Where did you find an anomaly that causes damage like a very curious HO? And you don’t really need treatment — you’ve already been healed. So where did this happen?
I told you, I got into an anomaly.
Why would I lie? First time seeing such an anomaly.
Why are you hiding something? Let’s not lie to each other, okay? I’ve seen guys like you plenty. They’re usually dead by then. That’s mercenary methods, kid. Where did you see the blue ones?

I didn’t see them. Found only one body. Had papers pointing to their stash. Found it, couldn’t open. Recently, while working on Vazgen, ran into bandits. One told me the stash and gave the code. The code worked. Then — you know. Surprise stash.
Familiar situation. Mercenary stash. Bandits who attack our collectors have access. Friend, don’t you feel you’re meddling in things not yours? Dealing with forces that could kill you anytime?
What are you talking about?
What do you mean?
Nothing. Just thinking aloud and giving advice. You’re smart, Varg, right? Don’t mess with mercenaries if you want to live. Got the hint or should I explain differently?
Do you work for the syndicate?

Think what I’d do if I worked for the syndicate and got caught? Probably shoot you now. But see — I’m talking to you. No, friend. I don’t work for them. But some of our people do business with them. I want to protect you. Varg, they’d eat you alive. Stop and mind your own business. You’ve been fishing for info on Clear Sky — keep doing that, don’t meddle here.
I can’t, friend. Everything’s connected. Pull one thread — the other follows. I’ll handle it myself. Thanks for your concern. I’ll manage if I’m lucky. Farewell.
Hey, Vazgen. I heard you’re looking for someone to handle a certain job. I can take it if it’s still relevant.

You, huh? Why not. Our guys, and many others, said you handle complex jobs pretty well. You did measurements, tested equipment. So I won’t give you simple tasks that anyone could handle. But I won’t throw you straight into serious work either — I have my trusted people for that. You may be a skilled specialist, but you still need to earn my trust.
So what now?
I’ll come back later. Not in the mood for your nonsense. If it’s a concrete job — speak up. Don’t drag it out...

Nothing. You’ll work. Specifically — I’m interested in artifact delivery in this dump. Everything else doesn’t matter. Deliveries, and the people gathering artifacts — that’s our priority. Don’t be fooled by me acting as a simple trader. No artifacts — no trade. Experienced collectors are valuable. We train the young, but…
Listen, can you be more specific? What exactly do you need from me?

Heh, impatient, huh? Why interrupt Vazgen? He should tell you everything. Long story short — an experienced collector, with the clan since its founding, got killed. Two bastards — literally their own men — shot him in the back. Took all the loot and hid nearby. Rumor is, a few more joined them — forming a small gang. Scouts tracked them. The problem is taking them out. Quickly and cleanly. Otherwise, this small gang could give us big trouble. They won’t hit the base, but they know collector routes. If they join a bigger gang — total disaster. So, get moving. Only one of our combat groups will cover you.
Alright, I’ll take this job gladly. I’ll tear those bastards apart.
Stalker, help… Got hit. Brother… medkit, I won’t forget this…
You think you’ll live another century, bastard? Tell me, how many of you were there?
Five of us. No one else. Me and my buddy tried to run when we heard the shooting. He wasn’t even a buddy — mercenary, I guess. He shot me on purpose and ran…
Where did he go? What exactly were you doing in this area?
Where did he head? What exactly were you doing here?
There’s a stash nearby. He was supposed to pick up an order from there… Heading to the farm now. Stalker, help, please. I’ve told you everything I know…
I’m already helping you…
No, bastard. I’d rather watch you die.
Well, you showed up, damn it. Ask your damn questions, let’s finish this fast…
Can’t wait to die, huh? Don’t worry — won’t hurt you much. Our talk is short. But only if you answer quickly and clearly. Don’t talk — you’ll die slow. Got it?
Yes, stalker. I’m not a mercenary. But I started working for them. Used to be free, not in a clan. Joined this gang for money. And I have something to tell.

Four questions. First — the stash you were going to for a job. I need the code. Second — how are the Blues connected to the local clan and whose body were they trying to pass as their dead fighter found on the farm? Third — what’s Hasan’s role? His name was in the documents I found. Last — is the Clear Sky clan connected to the mercenaries?

You asked a lot. I’ll tell what I know. Code for the stash: 5683. We weren’t there to take an order, but payment for a job. Mercenaries hire their own. Blues connected to the local clan via Hasan. Our task was to kill a collector carrying a rare item. Hasan tipped the mercs. We were only executors. Mercs were intermediaries. Hasan may smuggle extra artifacts through them — just my guess. Don’t know about the corpse. Clear Sky — heard mercs say they killed one nearby, not sure if true. That’s all. Stalker — I know you’ll kill me, do it fast.
Understood…
Job done. Bandits and mercs eliminated. One former free stalker among them. I interrogated him.
And what did he say? Don’t keep me waiting.
Vazgen, you’re a strange guy. Not Russian, but when you want, you speak with no accent at all.
Don’t talk around me. Tell me what he said, damn it…
Nothing special. Said they agreed to take out one of your collectors who recently grabbed a stash with a rare artifact. A mercenary tipped them off.
Nothing noteworthy. They agreed to take out a collector who recently found a rare artifact. A mercenary tipped them.

Alright, listen. Forget what he told you. It’s better for everyone. I’ll pay extra for your silence. Don’t worry — not with bullets, though it’d be cheaper. I’ll need you for another job. Don’t cause trouble. Also, see our artifact specialist — he might have more work. Once that’s sorted, come back to me.
Hope you weren’t lying.
So, what’s the other job you wanted to offer me?
Knew you’d be interested. You’re no simple man, Varg. This is basically a continuation of the matter you just handled.
Listening. What needs to be done now?
Maybe I’ll take it later. Not in the mood right now…

Here’s the deal. Main rule for our business to thrive — security. When security is threatened, nothing else matters. You missed one of the gang members, Varg. Now wise Vazgen has to figure out how to cover our asses.
Missed? There were only five. The bandits themselves told me there were five.
Missed? There were five. You took down four. Your people got the fifth. The bandits themselves confirmed there were five of them.

You believed them? Don’t disappoint me, friend. My scout spotted six. The sixth left an hour before you arrived at their hideout. You dropped three, two headed for the stash. One shot the other to distract you and tried to escape to the farm. That’s where the combat team caught him. Sixth slipped away from our territory unnoticed.
And now you want me to chase that bastard halfway across the Zone? How could I know there were six?

You didn’t know. Now you do. And now you know that bastard knew the gang’s plans, maybe even was their leader. If he joins a bigger gang, we have big problems. The artifact our man found — the Devil’s Egg. Only three ever found in the Zone. Worth a couple million. Think the bandits will want it? Of course they will.
What a mess. Fine — what exactly should I do?
Unexpected twist. So, what now?

That guy will likely meet renegade gangs. They’re reckless — no negotiations. Greedy, uncontrolled. We can redirect them. Take these documents and artifacts. Hide the documents in a stash — the info will reach the right ears. Deliver artifacts to another gang, say it’s from Vazgen. If they want more, they’ll contact Gogi. Then come back. Got it?
Do I need to know what’s in the documents?

No. But I’ll tell you. Guarantee you won’t run off with it — or we’ll kill you. Inside is a fake. One trusted gang member will be told the collector with the Devil’s Egg is on the swamps trying to sell via Clear Sky.
You’re setting up Clear Sky?

Yes. Nothing personal — purely business. Renegades will head to the swamps instead of here. They’ll make noise, find nothing, leave. All wins. I know you work for Clear Sky. Don’t worry. We help you in exchange. The gang you meet later is a decoy. Likely to try and kill you. Be ready to shoot back. We actually benefit if they attack you…
I don’t get why this meeting with the bandits is needed…
Not sure about your intentions…
Your logic is unclear. Why play all this out?
You don’t need to know why. Just play your part in the farce, that’s all.
Understood.
Hey, fool! Stop right there, runt. From Vazgen?
Yeah, from him. And you lot — who are you with?
Listen, fool. You don’t need to care. Got it? Hand over the artifacts and run if you want to live.
Go… to hell. You’re all dead anyway.
What did you say? You’re the dead one, runt. We’ll bring your head to Vazgen, got it?
Just try…
Here are the artifacts. Do I need to give something to Vazgen?
No, we’ll deliver your head ourselves.
Try it…
Hey, fool, you worthless runt, what’s all this crap supposed to mean? Were these bandits just hired to kill me?
Heh, you’re quick, I see. Well, nothing. Hope you realize you’ve learned too much unnecessary and dangerous info?
So you just decided to get rid of me?
You thought you could just dispose of me? Like that?

Well, you’re still alive, aren’t you? Because as soon as the bandits opened fire, someone took out two of them. Can you guess who or under whose guidance? No? Then shut up and listen. Not everything’s rosy here, stalker. You’ve stumbled into a huge mess, not easy to get out alive. So listen carefully…
So what exactly are you saying?

The clan founder, Harold, has a serious conflict with his deputy, Hasan. Hasan plays his own game and dreams of taking Harold’s place. Hasan is behind most of the strange things happening recently. He works with mercenaries, and they help him execute his plans. You’ve stumbled onto Hasan while investigating your Clear Sky assignment.
And what should I do? My main goal is to complete the assignment.

Two options. Since you survived the attempt, either help convince Harold that Hasan works only for himself, or offer your services to Hasan. You decide what fee to ask. Both can get you what you need. But understand the risk. Hasan won’t kill you publicly, and Harold can’t remove Hasan without proof.
Wait, how do you know all this?
And how do you know this?
How the hell did you find out about this?
Birds sing everywhere, my friend. You just need to know how to listen. Don’t worry about who Vazgen works for — just follow his advice. Next time, it won’t be simple bandits meeting you…
Understood. Thanks. Good luck…
Hello, Hasan. I need to have a business talk with you.
And hello to you. Come in, I’ve heard about your recent deeds. People here talk about nothing else.
I hope only good things?
At least good things, they say?

Maybe. I haven’t decided yet. They say you performed well working for the clan, and brought much benefit. But also that you started asking… risky questions. Questions that could bring trouble for both of us. They also say you survived opening a booby-trapped mercenary stash and easily dealt with some small gangs. You’re dangerous, Varg, and could be a valuable partner — or not. So, I’m listening…

Business partnership is good and essential. I’ve also heard a lot about you, Hasan. Some are unhappy you didn’t let them occupy local farms, and that you likely use this place as a meeting point with your mercenary allies. I also have documents confirming this. I’ve heard you hire small gang groups, guided by informers, to rob your own artifact collectors. This lets you sell artifacts bypassing the clan treasury. Dangerous business, isn’t it? Just thinking about what could happen if these rumors reach the wrong ears…

Now you start to please me… You know I can’t just kill you here. If you suddenly die, some stalker could give your documents to Harold — not good. Likewise, you can’t kill me. So, coming to me, you hope to ask for a higher price for your silence? Who hired you to work against me?

You’re almost right. Except one thing. I wasn’t hired against you. I don’t care about your clan and its schemes. But I can work for you and provide some services. These dangerous things became available while completing my Clear Sky assignment. My client wants to know the fate of their missing squad and the stalker scientist group that appeared here shortly before. I can forget what I know, and silence those who know it in exchange for full info on my assignment…

I see. This could change everything… Fine, if it all goes smoothly, I lose nothing and might even gain. Pure blackmail on your part, but I’d rather not choose the other option — less benefit for both. Agreed. You help eliminate all unhappy parties, and I’ll give you full info on the missing scientists and arrange a meeting with the clan contact.
Agreed.
Fine. I agree.

How do you propose to proceed? From what I understand, the scout leader — Harold’s trusted man — is in the way. His death should raise no suspicion. I’ve considered various plans: poisoning, clash with mercenaries, or with a rare mutant. Poisoning seems optimal. It cleans loose ends.

Exactly. I know how to do it discreetly. Tell the scout to wait in the designated area — further instructions and equipment will come from me. Hint it’s for testing a new batch of gas masks at the anomaly cluster. Your supplier or mechanic should get me the necessary components.
My mechanic will prepare everything needed. I see you plan to poison using gas masks. I considered that, but traces may remain. My men will monitor the operation.
No traces, nobody will figure it out. If the prepped catalyst works perfectly. Prepare the info I need. When I return, we’ll discuss it.
Hi. From Hasan. You should know what I need.
Ah, I see. Well, yes — I’m aware. But I have some questions.
What questions?
Questions?

The gas masks are clear. Filters are available. But other components… I don’t fully understand their purpose. Special substances act as catalysts. Multiple components may serve as catalysts, depending on the task.
Understood. I need hydroquinone in a capsule for spraying.
Ah, I see. Clear now. Here you go. Regards to Hasan — tell him everything is done perfectly.
Will do.
Hey, we meet again.
Hello. So, you’re working for Hasan now? Didn’t expect that. Any extra instructions?
Not much, just a one-time assignment. I’m a simple courier. Give them the new batch of gas masks, let them run through the anomaly cluster. They’ll buy a bigger batch later.

If that’s true, fine. I thought you became one of his trusted men. By the way, new info: remember the mercenary corpse? I told you it was a Clear Sky member. Indeed, Hasan’s people eliminated him. He was in the squad you searched for. Fate of the rest unknown.
Good. Thanks. Be careful in the anomalies. Better go to the chemical anomaly nearby.
Okay. Be careful in the anomalies. Better go to the chemical one nearby.
We’ll go there. Bye. Don’t get too close to Hasan. Harold already has some leads on him. Soon he’ll expose him, and heads will roll. Watch your own.
I’ll try. Bye.
It’s done. The scout group is eliminated. Did your observers confirm it?
Yes, Varg. I’ve already received confirmation. Good job. I must admit, I didn’t expect such cold calculation from you.
And what else didn’t you expect?
And what else could you not expect?
I didn’t expect that you’d come to me instead of going to Harold.
Yeah. And I thought you wouldn’t expect me to survive after opening the stash…
Not without reason. But we don’t choose another person’s path, we only guide them. Alright, let’s focus on your task. I’m eager to end this story once and for all.
Yes, let’s do it.
Hey. I have very important information that will interest you.
Hello. I’ve heard about your recent deeds, Varg. You’ve provided serious assistance to our faction. I hope your information is truly important if you decided to contact me directly?

More than that. It concerns your deputy, Hasan. Most likely, he’s playing his own game here. He’s somehow involved with mercenaries recently spotted by your scouts. Most likely, he’s selling stolen artifacts through them. Additionally, under his leadership, several bandit groups operated, collecting these artifacts from your gatherers following Hasan’s orders. So mercenaries, bandits — all links in a chain leading to Hasan.

Hmm, stalker. I suspected something like this, but not this much. Hasan has indeed changed recently. Even without your words, we had suspicions he was involved in recent events. But that the “rat” was Hasan himself? Hard to believe. I need solid proof supporting your claims. I can’t just accuse him of betrayal and shoot him — it would split the clan and ruin our business. But with proof of his guilt, everything changes. Also, what’s in it for you? Why help me and expose your deputy?

Simple: a favor for a favor. I’m not here for your clan’s affairs. Recently, a group of scientists disappeared in your controlled area, along with the Clear Sky squad sent to find them. I need full info, and I think only you can provide it. About Hasan — I have some evidence. Some documents, and my conclusions are shared by your scout commander. He suspected Hasan colluded with mercenaries after being denied access to farms. Most likely he used them for his purposes. Later, I found a mercenary corpse there. I also interrogated a bandit who claimed a high-ranking clan member passed info on your gatherers’ movements.
You say the scout leader? Well, he can’t confirm any of your concerns anymore.
Why is that?

He died with his group under very strange circumstances. Their whole team was found dead near an anomaly. They were testing new equipment — gas masks from the supply batch. We don’t know what killed them. Or rather, who — the Zone, or were humans involved? So, as you said, if the Cripple was in Hasan’s way, he might be involved in their deaths. Logical? I think yes. So we need to look for traces. Maybe a psi-blast, poison gas, or something else. Three skilled scouts couldn’t just die in a chemical anomaly.
Alright, I’ll take the investigation… I already have some ideas. I’ll need equipment. Which of your people can I rely on?
Go to the supplier then. He’s fully my man. Do everything properly, and then we’ll talk about the scientists and their feathered friends.
Alright. Bye.
Understood. Bye.
Hello. I’m from Harold. He said you could assist me fully in a delicate matter. First, tell me where you gave the latest batch of gas masks? They were issued to the deceased scouts for field tests.
To our technicians. They prepared them for distribution among clan fighters. Why? Does it matter?
Yes, it matters. Also, I need special equipment — a spectrometer. I know it’s rare, but still. You don’t sell artifacts cheaply here either.
True. I have a spectrometer, but why do you need it? What are you planning to investigate?
The place where your scouts died.
The place where your scouts perished.
Wait, so you think their death wasn’t an accident?
I don’t think, I want to find out. Thanks for the equipment. I’ll return it intact to Harold. Bye.
Hey there, tech. You don’t want to tell me anything?
What’s with the attitude? I owe you nothing. Go away.
I think you will owe, and not a little. I’m from Harold. Now, tell me — did you give the scout leader the gas mask batch for testing?
Yes, I did. What’s the problem? The batches are usually given to scouts for preliminary testing. What’s wrong?

The masks were contaminated with specific poisons, which reacted when stalkers approached the anomaly. Traces are minimal, but present. Another day and I’d be too late. But I can confirm you prepared the contaminated batch that killed the scouts. Decide: confess and name the contractor, and you die quickly. Act tough — I won’t envy you.
I didn’t give them anything. I just checked filters and issued a spare set. Not to them directly, but to a local courier, just running errands.
Whose man is the courier — Hasan’s or Harold’s?
Wait, whose man was the courier?

He’s Hasan’s man. Listen, Varg — don’t kill me, I’ll tell everything. Hasan arranged it all. He wanted to eliminate the Cripple. He interfered too much. I’ll give up his accomplices in the clan. I swear.
What accomplices? Stalkers or who?
Stalkers. They have a meeting place. A shared stash with Hasan. I’ll give you the coordinates. Just don’t kill me.
Fine, live. But you’ll breathe and work as I say. Tell Hasan I was here — you know the hint. Sit, scum, dry your pants.
So, I’ve managed to figure everything out.
Well, don’t keep me waiting. Speak up if you have something to say.

As you suspected, the scouts were killed. They were poisoned using rather rare chemical substances. Half of it was in the filters of their gas masks. The catalyst reacted near the anomaly.
