Within a day, all traces would have evaporated and no one would have found anything. I tracked that Hasan’s man handed the gas masks to the group. The technician swears he didn’t tamper with them — he’s lying. He gave up all of Hasan’s accomplices in the clan. When I tried approaching them, they opened fire. I had to eliminate them. I also uncovered their stash, and the records found there prove Hasan’s direct guilt.
Excellent news, Varg. That traitor got what he deserved. We can grab him by the collar and shoot him. The faction’s fighters won’t question anything anymore.
What about the technician? He’s Hasan’s man. Possibly not the only one. Surely there are others involved in this scheme.
And what about the rest of his people? Should we deal with them as well?

That’s less important. The main thing is to decapitate the enemy. The other pawns can’t do much anymore. And I won’t find a new technician quickly, so let him live for now. If needed, he’ll follow Hasan. Alright, I need to give orders and prepare Hasan’s elimination. And I might still need you for that.
Good. But now it’s time to deal with my matter.
You’re my contact? May I ask why you’ve been out of touch for so long?
No, I’m a Monolith contact. Or do you see anyone else here? Why would I contact you? Have you found the scientists or our missing group? I don’t think so. So why should I listen to your whining?
I uncovered some information quite quickly... I needed to report it and hoped for new instructions.

New instructions? My friend, you’re not in the army. I’m not a sergeant, you’re not a conscript. You were given a task, you accepted it. The result is what matters, nothing more. No one will hold your hand or breathe down your neck. How you achieve it doesn’t matter to me. To our leadership — even less. Result. Period. You can bend the whole local stalker faction over if that’s what it takes — we don’t care. I need data, facts, preferably the specific people we’re looking for. Alive or dead — doesn’t matter. Result. Since you contacted me on behalf of someone, you have something. So spill what you’ve found.

Alright. I managed to find one of the missing scientists. He died in the anomaly. He had some documents that might interest you. Also, I tracked at least two people from your faction. One, probably an agent like me, died under mysterious circumstances — locals say eaten by unknown mutants. True or not — unknown. The other stalker, likely a fighter from the missing group, also died or was killed, most likely killed, either by locals or mercenaries under local orders. I might have mixed up who was the agent and who was the fighter. But both are dead — fact. Since they were here, the missing group likely didn’t leave the territory. I suspect it was entirely wiped out. Locals might be hiding something. Regarding the scientists, there’s a presumed area where they might still be if alive.

Understood. Overall, a satisfactory outcome for now. On dry land, even a crayfish is fish. You’re right about our “regular” agent. From your info, he’s dead. We feared this. He hadn’t contacted us for a long time. Last time, we learned he found one of the missing group fighters. The last thread to this group breaks with his death. Most likely, they all perished. That’s our conclusion, though grim. The fact that you know a more specific area for continuing the scientist search is encouraging. The fate of the scientists is crucial to us. Here’s what we’ll do: several of our groups will arrive soon. You’ve gained the trust of local stalkers. By any means necessary, convince them not to interfere with our squads. Then, you’ll indicate the search area to our teams. Understood? Alright, stay in touch. We’ll contact you when the squads are ready for operation.
Understood. Bye.
Well, hello, savior. Why did you need me?
Don’t you have a clue yourself?
Probably to pay a debt. But I have nothing to give you now. I haven’t searched for artifacts much, my main stash with anything valuable is on Agroyantar...
I owe you, and now you decided to collect the debt… though I have no idea how I could repay it — no money or artifacts here. All the valuable stuff is in my Agroyantar stash.
Think about what I might need from you. Money, artifacts — fine, but I don’t need them. Working for me — that you can do. Handle a couple of jobs and consider the debt paid.
What exactly do you need from me? And whose interests will these jobs serve? You’re not a simple stalker, right?

Not simple, like you. Interests? Well… first, yours. Second — the Freedom faction. Third — you’ll decide yourself. Enough chit-chat. Here’s what I need: I recently got info about a large bandit squad here. Local stalkers somehow negotiated with them and let them into their territory. These bandits might be linked to mercenaries, who in turn may be linked to stalkers. The point: these bandits are a major nuisance. That’s all you need to know. And, as you guessed, your task is to eliminate them.
These bandits are likely linked to Hasan, the local deputy. Strange, Harold promised to eliminate him. Well, whatever. How many of them are there and where are they located?

Why assume only Hasan could be linked to the bandits? Good questions — I like that approach. But you’re jumping to conclusions. Their squad is no more than ten armed men. Soon they’ll settle near an abandoned local farm. You have a few hours to prepare a proper ambush. I’ll give you stash coordinates — there’s some equipment. Collect a couple of explosives and meet your guests appropriately. After this job, we’ll meet again and discuss more. I’m sure you’ll have questions.
Can’t we avoid questions? Also, I need to negotiate with locals...
We don’t need unnecessary questions. Solve this so that none arise. Also, I need to negotiate with locals…

About the Clear Sky faction — yes, I’m aware. Working for me won’t take much time. Also, you might reconsider some plans. As you see, my answer raises more questions. Questions always arise, my friend. What matters is whether it makes sense to ask them…
You think it won’t make sense?
I don’t think. What matters is what you think. Alright, bye. Once you finish the job, contact me again. Then we’ll see if you find meaning or I’ll have to point it out.
Alright.
And what am I supposed to make of this, you damn fool? I’m dead now. I had a contract with these feathered stalkers. And you, bastard, just set me up...
Calm down. I told you questions would arise. And as I see it, you won’t be able to find the answers on your own...

What are you even talking about, damn it? Yes, I’m grateful you saved my life, but now you’re throwing me into the noose yourself... Freedom decided to ignite a conflict with the Clear Sky faction through my hands? For what? And what am I supposed to do now? I was almost done with my work for the feathered ones before you messed it up.

Think, my friend. Your head wasn’t given to you just to wear a gas mask. And I’m not shoving you into the noose — on the contrary, for the second time, I’m pulling you out. You just don’t see it yet. Tell me, do you really think your contract with the Clear Sky faction will be generously paid? Do you think they need extra witnesses to their affairs? You were hired for this task and will be removed as soon as it’s done. The Clear Sky folks don’t yet know their squad was eliminated by you — but they will. For now, we have time...
What? You think their contract is fake? There hasn’t been a single case where someone could doubt their reputation.

Yes, you’re right. There hasn’t. And do you realize why? Because there was no one to tell. Stalker, I don’t wander this Zone for nothing. And we don’t run our affairs in vain. There’s always a hidden war between factions. And now you’re caught up to the hilt. So listen to what wise people say, if you don’t want to be out of the game. In it, you either win or die. There’s no third option. So decide what you really need.
Why should I trust you?
Name at least one reason why I should trust you.

Um, probably because you have no choice now. You destroyed a combat squad from one of the influential factions in the Zone. And now it’s up to me whether we solve this problem or not. But think about it. Clear Sky sends a free stalker off to God-knows-where to find their group of scientists. Haven’t you wondered what this group is, why they were attacked, and why a Clear Sky squad was sent after them? Or why now a whole combat squad of ruthless thugs is sent after you? By the way, I forgot to ask — how did it go? Did the ambush work?
The ambush worked. The scientists were working for Clear Sky. They were attacked by mercenaries, a clan combat squad tried to save them, but failed. Is this version not good enough for you?

You see, life isn’t that simple. Let’s start with the fact that these stalker scientists never worked for Clear Sky. You’ve been deceived, my friend. Right from the start. I understand you’ll want proof… Go to the abandoned tower. A stalker will be there waiting. He’s not my man, and he shouldn’t know about me. But his info will confirm everything I said. Tell him you were looking for the missing group of scientists on behalf of Freedom. This stalker is well-known in the Zone. You won’t be able not to believe him. At least, I hope so. And I hope you won’t do anything stupid. Hurry. Time is short. Once you understand the situation, you’ll likely want to help me resolve it your way.
I hope you’re right...

The ambush failed. I screwed up with the explosives. The scientists worked for Clear Sky. They were attacked by mercenaries, a clan combat squad tried to save them, but apparently failed. Is this version not good enough for you?

You see, life isn’t that simple. Let’s start with the fact that these stalker scientists never worked for Clear Sky. You’ve been deceived, my friend. Right from the start. I understand you’ll want proof… Go to the abandoned tower. A stalker will be there waiting. He’s not my man, and he shouldn’t know about me. But his info will confirm everything I said. Tell him you were looking for the missing group of scientists on behalf of Freedom. This stalker is well-known in the Zone. You won’t be able not to believe him. At least, I hope so. And I hope you won’t do anything stupid. Hurry. Time is short. Once you understand the situation, you’ll likely want to help me resolve it your way.
I hope you’re right...
Fang? And what are you doing here?
Do we know each other? I recall you, but I don’t recognize you yet. Are you one of the locals?
No. I’m Varg, from Agroyantar. Your group, along with Strelok and Ghost, was there some time ago when I was still working for Orest...
Now I understand. But why are you here?
I’m looking for the missing group of stalker scientists on behalf of Freedom. They recently appeared here. Some mercenaries or bandits harassed them, and then they came here...
What? So we’re working on the same thing. Quite a surprise. How did you track me down? Tell me everything you know.

An old friend told me where to find you. I don’t know much. A Clear Sky squad was sent after them, probably to help. But they likely died here. I haven’t found any traces of the scientists yet. What do you know about this?

I know more than you, stalker. But I don’t want to fully fill you in yet. Though if you survive, you’ll uncover most of it yourself. Listen. The group of scientists left Yantra and were supposed to meet us, that is, Strelok. We arranged some equipment for their journey to the Zone’s center. We didn’t hand it over in Yantra — too many prying eyes and ears. The meeting was set on neutral ground. But the scientists never made it. Someone got wind of the deal and tried to stop us. And these someones were bastards from Clear Sky. They didn’t want anyone to cross the Burner and would do anything to stop us. They attacked the scientists, but something went wrong. Most of the group died, but those carrying the equipment escaped. The feathered ones aren’t great fighters and didn’t hire mercenaries. A Clear Sky combat squad was sent to find and destroy the remaining scientists and their equipment at all costs...
Wow, what a twist. I had info that the scientists actually worked for Clear Sky and that a squad was sent to help them...

Ha, that’s all lies. Trust me. Clear Sky are the ones responsible. They’d dig up the entire Zone to find the scientists’ equipment. But it’s already out of their reach. I recently took it and hid it safely. So Clear Sky ends up with nothing. I’m sorry we couldn’t get the scientists out. The feathered ones will kill them when they find them. But we must accept this for the greater good and stalker cause. Perhaps Freedom or Duty will reach them first.
And you’re not afraid to tell me all this?
I don’t understand why you’re telling me all this.

What should I fear? The fate of the scientists no longer concerns me. They did their part — delivered the equipment. The feathered ones won’t get it anyway. Even if you’re their spy, it benefits me — you can’t kill me, and if you pass on the info I gave you, it gives the scientists a chance. Also, I might have lied to you.

Damn, this is complicated. What mess am I in... And yet I lied to you, Fang. I actually worked — and still work — for Clear Sky. They hired me to find the stalker scientists and their missing squad. Recently, Freedom people approached me. They hinted that Clear Sky would use me and kill me after the job. I didn’t believe it at first, but now you’ve fully confirmed it. Moreover, Freedom set it up so I killed several Clear Sky members I was supposed to work with. So I’m trapped in the gears, in a web of lies with no escape.

Yeah, stalker. Quite the story… Your situation is grim. You can’t return to the feathered ones — they’ll find out you killed their people. Freedom set you up… I wouldn’t trust them after this. And you lied to me at first too. Kid, this is a game. A dangerous game where you either survive or die. No third option.
I’ve heard this somewhere before...
Familiar words you speak...

By the way, we ended up here and were indirectly connected earlier. Remember that stalker Vazgen? You delivered a document package from him to a stash. Well, I’m the one who took it and forwarded it to the bandits. That caused big problems for Clear Sky. Soon, dozens of renegades will flood the swamps. This might help us and Strelok and distract them while we prepare our mission. And here’s my advice — try to escape or flee to Duty. Not optimal, but at least they don’t betray their own. If Freedom offers reasonable cooperation, why not? They didn’t kill you. The main thing — you didn’t side with the feathered freaks. And that should be your main focus now. Decide for yourself. We can’t take you with us — we have our own path.
One Freedom member saved my life. Maybe the same person you worked with. I’ll try to escape first. If that fails, we’ll see.
Exactly. If you can’t escape, go to Freedom without hesitation. Alright, take care. And forget everything we discussed.
Alright. Good luck to your group. Perhaps you’re the ones who will change the Zone’s history forever.
I don’t even know where to start. Fang completely turned my understanding of what’s happening upside down.
Upside down enough that you tried to run?
But you didn’t run...
After all, I’m still here...
Correct. The veil of lies that had clouded your eyes didn’t prevent you from thinking clearly. Had you ignored my message, you would already be dead.

I understand that. Alright, since we’re here — I’m still of use to you and you’re not going to kill me just yet. What should I do now in light of recent events? As I understand it, our main concern is the Clear Sky faction?

You understand correctly. Clear Sky is a big problem, and we need to deal with it. Officially, I represent Freedom. Unofficially — you’ll find out when the time comes. Or maybe not. That’s not important now. What matters is that you will work for me for the time being. You’ve proven yourself here — you’re smart and capable of manipulating faction affairs. I’ve been watching you for a long time — and you might have observed my actions, even without realizing it was me involved. So, here’s what we need to do. First, eliminate the Clear Sky members who came here after the stalker scientists. You’ve already taken out one group, but there are still informants and a major combat squad remaining. Second, we need to take care of the scientists themselves.
What exactly do you propose regarding the scientists?
How do you suggest we handle the scientists?

There are two options here. Either we eliminate them, or we use them further in our play against Clear Sky. Most likely we’ll need to eliminate them. Extracting them from here would be difficult. They’ve already completed their main task — delivering the equipment to Strelok’s group.
Your organization supports Strelok’s group?
Why does your organization support Strelok’s group?
What benefit do you gain from supporting Strelok and his stalkers?

Let’s say our organization currently benefits from Strelok and his team. They’re trying to penetrate the Zone’s center, where no normal human has stepped for a long time. Fine, let them try. As long as they remain a thorn in Clear Sky’s side, we’ll play hand in hand with them.
You said you’ve been watching me for a long time. And that you were involved in many of the events here...

Correct. I was the missing Freedom scout you tried to find upon arrival. At the same time, I was also an informant for Duty. In this way, I removed unnecessary participants from the game — not permanently, but for a time. And that time, it was you acting through my hands, even if you didn’t realize it. I also sabotaged one of the stalkers’ radio stations, nudged Hasan to cooperate with mercenaries, persuaded Vazgen to prepare significant evidence against Clear Sky. Much more, my friend. A big chess game started here, likely leading to a major clan war. As grim as it sounds, we need this war. And you have an important role in it, if you continue working as you do now.
Why did you send the renegades to the swamps? I don’t think it was just to cause trouble for Clear Sky.

Correct. I had much more far-reaching plans. We’ll talk about that later. For now, you need to finish things here. Go to the designated area first. There you’ll kill the Clear Sky agent. You’ve met him here before. Then go to the stalkers’ base. Find their radio operator and tell him the time for Operation “Retribution” has come. He’ll understand. That’s a signal for contacting the feathered squad. Afterwards, you eliminate them. Lastly… The group of scientists still alive is too large — six people. I’ll take three and lead them away, explaining why later. You’ll enter their hideout and eliminate the rest. Understood?
Understood. I’m on my way.
Valar Morghulis... Have you already forgotten me?
Same to you. I thought you had decided to leave these lands. And you did.
I thought so too, but circumstances turned out differently... I have something for you.
Circumstances, my friend. You know they’re not always on our side. By the way, I have something for you.
What exactly?
A mutual friend asked me to pass along that the time for Operation “Retribution” has come. You understand what I mean?
Perfectly… When you see our mutual friend, tell him everything was executed perfectly.
Good. Farewell.
It’s done. The Clear Sky members who came here — all eliminated. Their informant, the combat squad — all gone. The scientists you didn’t extract have also been successfully dealt with.

Excellent. This round has ended in our favor for now. The feathered got what was coming, Strelok’s group is ready to head to the center for the Burner. Duty and Freedom each lost a squad, temporarily removing them from play. Local stalkers — we prevented the faction from collapsing, that’s also good. So what next? Now we need to play the next round, with much higher stakes.
So you’re not a Freedom member? Won’t you tell me who you really are?
Clearly not Freedom. Will you stop playing riddles and tell me who you work for?
Ah, my friend, I wouldn’t advise asking that again. Let’s say — I am someone who cares about what happens in this world...
Fine. As you wish. You saved my life, and I’m grateful for that. What happened to the scientists you extracted from here?
They will be taken care of properly. A mercenary agreed to escort them to safety. By the way, I’ll tell you a bit about my plans.
I’m listening carefully...
I’m listening...

The mercenary leading the remaining scientists isn’t an ordinary person. You share some similarities with him. His name is Scar. Perhaps you’ve heard the name. Like you, he doesn’t yet realize how or why he will be used in the upcoming game. But one day, he will play a key role. For now, it’s important that he reaches the swamps as quickly as possible.
What is he supposed to do in the swamps, especially with scientists that Clear Sky will just kill?

Who said the feathered will recognize whether those scientists are the right ones? Forget about the scientists entirely. They will all die. Once Scar reaches the swamps, there will be a strong and sudden emission. No one will survive except him. Don’t be surprised. Remember the scout injured by the psi-wave in the village? That was also my doing. Scar will end up with Clear Sky. He will be recruited, just like you were once. Lebedev’s goal will be the same — stop Strelok from reaching Pripyat and the Chernobyl NPP. And Scar will carry out their will. Initially, they’ll likely order him to clear the swamps of renegades you sent, then we’ll see… The important thing is, he will be one of them.
And then? He starts working for the feathered, tracking Strelok’s group. How does that benefit us?

Our benefit is that we’ll know the plans of both Clear Sky and Strelok’s group. Remember — our goal in the next round is to destroy Clear Sky completely. We’ll make Scar lure them out of their lair and eliminate them. Some we’ll kill, some we might spare and force to work for us. That doesn’t matter. The important thing is their faction must cease to exist. And that’s just one part of the plan.
Fine, and what’s my role?
And what role is assigned to me?

It’s simple, my friend. We’ll set the war in motion… Or rather, we’ll nudge the factions into action. New territories will open, clans will go there for new artifacts. Their patrons will try to negotiate, dividing spheres of influence. We’ll just add some spice… First, we deal with Duty and Freedom. That will be easiest. Especially since you’ve already taken the first steps here...
Fine. You’ve shown me another path for a deceived man. You can count on me — I won’t stray. But I didn’t say you’d be walking alongside me...
Greetings. You wanted to see me? What happened and why do you need me?
And my respects to you, old friend. It's not proper to forget who owes whom, and you owe me quite a lot — namely, your life. I called you here so that you could repay that debt.
Ask for whatever you wish — artifacts, money. I’ll give it. But right now I need to finish a task I committed to before our meeting. I’ll earn a little cash, so I can settle my debt to you.
Always about money. I don’t need your money. I need you to work for me. And breaking your contract with the Clear Sky faction will be the first thing you do for me.
What? I don’t understand.
Don’t understand? What do you mean?

Exactly what you heard. You will break your contract with the Feathered Stalkers while it’s not too late. You don’t know the whole truth, my friend. I will reveal it to you, and you must believe it. Your life depends on it.
Alright, I’m listening.

Clear Sky hired you to find the missing group of scientists and their search team. But you never asked yourself who attacked these scientists and why a Clear Sky squad was sent after them. I’ll tell you — it was Clear Sky themselves. The so-called search team was actually a punitive unit tasked with tracking down the surviving scientists and eliminating them. They deceived you, Varg. You would find the scientists, report back to Clear Sky, and then be killed along with them.
These are terrible things you say. I don’t believe you.
Complete nonsense. I don’t trust your words.

Your choice, Varg. Go meet your employers. They are expecting you. But think — would I have saved your life back then if I intended to lie to you now? Either way, if you change your mind — I’ll be nearby.
We’ll see.
Hello. Hasan said you’d be waiting. Why the delay in contact?
Why contact you before you found anything? Now that you have news — speak. We’ll pay you and go our separate ways.
Pay me? Already? The scientists haven’t even been found yet.
Why suddenly be so generous if the contract isn’t finished? The scientists aren’t found.
So should we leave? Why contact us then? Speak — what have you discovered?

I found that the scientists did reach territory controlled by local stalkers. Your squad followed but most likely perished. Several pieces of evidence confirm this. The bodies of deceased Clear Sky members were found. As for the scientists, an approximate location is determined. We can head there directly.
Good. Not much, but it’s something.
It seemed to me that the news of the area to search for your hideout isn’t exactly news anymore?
Right. This isn’t news. So I think we no longer need your services. You did well and earned your reward.
One question — why, knowing exactly where to search, did you act only now?
And why, knowing where they can be found, did you act only now?
Ah, you’re sharp... We needed to make sure you would complete what remained, cover where needed, and then our need for you would vanish... Sorry, but that’s the truth. Best regards from Hasan...
Bastards...
You were right. Clear Sky deceived me and tried to kill me. And Hasan too...

What did I say? But you didn’t want to listen. No matter — you’re alive and can fix things. Your main mistake wasn’t trusting the Feathered, but interfering in the local stalker group’s internal conflict on the wrong side. Hasan is vile. You knew what he did and still chose to support him. He told Clear Sky everything you should have told. Your services became redundant and they decided to eliminate you before the scientists were even found. However, Hasan didn’t issue a direct order to kill you. So he influenced your fate indirectly. The same cannot be said about the faction’s fate. Soon he will kill Harold, if he hasn’t already, and will exert full control. We’ll deal with that later.
What do you suggest?
And how should we act in this case?
We must act, my friend. The situation can still be turned in our favor, despite your misstep.
Who do you really work for, and why did Clear Sky want to kill those scientists?

Officially, I work for Freedom. That’s enough for now. Unofficially, I work for a far more powerful organization. Consider yourself part of it. Refusing isn’t an option. For Clear Sky you are an enemy, for Freedom you will also be an enemy, as with Duty. Remember your arrival here — I was the Freedom scout you were looking for, and also an informant for Duty. I orchestrated their attack on Freedom. You already dealt with Duty. I can feed this info where needed. So, the choice is yours. I’m not scaring you — I want another skilled ally. You’ve proven yourself investigating. I’ve been watching your progress. The corpse for the mercs, the wounded psionic scout, the broken radio station — that’s just some of my work. So...
I’ve made my choice. I’m with you. But why did the Feathered need to kill the scientists?

Here’s the explanation. You know stalkers like Strelok, Fang, and Ghost? They’re near-legendary in the Zone. They could become greater when they execute their plan through the Exterminator to Pripyat and Chernobyl NPP. These scientists were to provide them equipment. But Strelok’s journey has enemies. Clear Sky is among them. They attacked the scientists to prevent the delivery. Something went wrong, and they didn’t kill them all. Some escaped here. They sent punitive units after them, but those perished. That’s the whole story.
No one has ever crossed the Exterminator.
But that’s impossible. No one has ever passed through the Exterminator.

Just because no one has yet doesn’t mean it’s impossible. Our organization benefits if Strelok and his team succeed. They’re also a thorn for our enemies. Enough talk — later I’ll answer your other questions. For now, here’s the plan: I know where the Feathered squad is stationed. Attacking them directly would be foolish. You go to the base, find Hasan, and tell him the mercs he worked with were killed by Clear Sky. He’ll send them to the village. Then they’ll handle it. You show up afterward to deal with the survivors. I’ll cover traces at the base and decide what to do with the scientists.
What’s wrong with the faction base?
Hasan will be in charge now. That’s the problem. You’ve also been too exposed there — not good for upcoming operations. We’ll have to remove them.
Understood. I’ll do everything.
Valar Morghulis. Thought you’d never see me again? Hoping Clear Sky would kill me?
What are you talking about? You’re not that easy to kill, stalker. And I see — the Feathered have stopped being your friends. Unexpected, huh? What brings you here?

But they probably won’t be your friends either. I’ve learned something here that you’ll find interesting. Two mercenaries you were dealing with were recently killed. Don’t ask how I know — I just do. They were killed by Clear Sky, avenging the poor guy who was torn apart by the Blues and passed off as one of their own. The squad responsible stopped in the village today...
Ha. And you think I’ll believe that? Planning to deal with your former friends with my own hands? No way, stalker...

Tell me, what will your partners do when they learn you know why their men died but did nothing? They’ll likely be upset and question all agreements. Doing business with someone who keeps critical info secret — that’s bad, right? Not good, Hasan.

I understand your hint. Fine, I’ll tell my mercenary friends that Clear Sky occupied the village. They’ll take care of them. How many times have you tried to make me look foolish? Be careful, stalker — my patience isn’t endless. Soon Harold will be gone, and no one will save you.
Don’t worry. That was the last time. I’m sure of it. Farewell.

Everything was done as you commanded. Mercenaries and Clear Sky members are dead. The stalker base destroyed, local clan gone. The scientists in the bunker are dead too. Now tell me — what was the point of this madness?

For our common good. Clear Sky deceived you and used you in their selfish plans, but they failed. Ours are now underway. While you dealt with Clear Sky, I extracted some scientists and handed them to a mercenary named Scar. He’ll lead three scientists to the Swamps, to the Clear Sky base. Allegedly, to pass them through Agroyantar. Those we no longer need will die along the way. The mercenary will infiltrate the Feathered as a Trojan horse.
Understood. You want to plant your man to undermine the faction internally, but wouldn’t sending renegades to the Swamps do it better?

Luring the renegades from their lair was a wise move with far-reaching consequences. They’ll weaken the Feathered for a time, but not destroy them. Relying on mere thieves is foolish — expendable material. Scar will likely first deal with renegades, then pursue Strelok’s group. Clear Sky must stop Strelok, and we must stop Clear Sky. We’ll exploit their mutual weaknesses.
What will my role be?
What will I need to do?

You’ll go with me. Before Scar reaches the Swamps, we’ll push the factions into a more overt war. You’ve proven yourself a skilled agent and can infiltrate Duty or Freedom ranks. Starting the conflict will be easy.
Why is this necessary?
And why exactly?
The Zone needs a shake-up. Many clans have gained power and influence, not all working for our organization. They need weakening.
You’re not telling everything...
Of course. But you don’t need to worry too much. I’ve briefed you on our plan. Other clans — don’t worry, they’ll clash among themselves. The main task — eliminate Clear Sky. Completely.
Understood… You can count on me. I guess we should leave this place.
Don’t you get tired of standing here all day?
Heh, my friend, everyone in the clan has their job. Mine is to stand and guard the base from unwanted guests like you… If you have nothing concrete, better go your way and don’t interfere.
Take it easy. I’m not from here, don’t know your rules. Just arrived. What can you tell me about this place?

Stalker rules are the same everywhere. You’re not a rookie, and I see through any pretense. You didn’t come here just for money or artifacts. So I warn you — don’t cause trouble. If you do, you won’t live long.
I’ve heard this before. Don’t threaten people without reason. They didn’t insult you. Though, you could tell a weary traveler something interesting — maybe a recent rumor or event.
Did I threaten you? Call me friend again — I’ll slit your throat. That’s a threat. Notice the difference?
More than enough.
I think so.

Good. Rarely come understanding people. Recently two arrived — from Duty and Freedom. Their squads found the remains nearby. Who convinced them — nobody knows. Security’s on edge. Better go and don’t ask questions.
Heard anything else interesting besides Freedom-Duty clashes?

You’re persistent. Fine, a story from wandering Military stalkers: a scientist’s corpse was found in the forests. How it got there — unknown. From the body, stalkers took a decent protective suit and approached dangerous anomalies. One anomaly held an artifact worth nearly half a million. What happened after — unknown. Those who found it disappeared. The stash they might have left is unfound. Running the forests alone? No takers. Likely the artifact’s in someone powerful’s hands now.
Well, an informative chat. Pure nonsense, if not for the scientist’s corpse… Alright, I’m leaving. Good luck.
Hey there. Why are you sitting around, slacking off? I’m Varg, by the way, just arrived here recently.
Call me Chekh. Just Chekh. I just finished my shift and took a seat to rest. Also, the boss gave me a task different from simple guard duty.
Got it. You been here long? And who do the patrols report to in the clan? Thinking about joining you.

I’ve only been here a couple of weeks. Came on business, but got delayed. Needed work that’s not too messy. Harold recruits people for base security personally, so you can’t just walk in. Only through someone, or for a specific reason… Varg, you ask too many questions, drawing unwanted attention. Not professional, colleague.
Colleague? How did you know that…

Relax. This place is full of agents and people of different people. We’re all connected, so you can speak more openly than you originally intended. I assume your questions would have been about the fate of the missing Clear Sky squad and stalker scientists they were following? Locals know a bit about that. And they have contacts to your employers. But they won’t lift a finger without benefit. If you deliver them a benefit, they’ll hand you everything on a silver platter. Be careful. You’re watched not only by your employers and locals, but also by other clans. I can share more detailed info for a service. Your choice. I need help placing special equipment. Will you help me install a signal jammer or a radar blocker? So, ready for closer cooperation?
Why help me out of any love for mankind? Well, I lose nothing. Alright, I’ll help with the signal jammer. But I need details. And how do I know you’re not one of those representatives?

From nowhere. Believe if you want, don’t if you don’t. Simple. But take advice — don’t blindly trust some faction members you meet. Their motives aren’t always selfless. Especially some from Freedom. About the jammer — I need to secure the area for confidential meetings of some agents on the farm. The device is disguised as an old crate. Place it and return.
Alright, got it. You work for Duty?
So you work for the Duty faction?
No, I don’t. You won’t know who I work for until I choose to tell. For now, I just want to protect you from the web of a very skilled spider. He’s here now disguised as a Freedom member.
Even so — I don’t care. My business is with Clear Sky. The rest doesn’t concern me. Well, now it does — specifically the info you’re willing to share.
As you wish. But note, later may be too late.
Noted…
How do I know you’re not one of those representatives? Well, I lose nothing. Alright, I’ll help with the blocker — we’ll see. But I need details.

From nowhere. Believe or not. Simple. But take advice — don’t blindly trust faction members you meet. Their motives aren’t always selfless. Especially some Freedom members. About the blocker — someone might sabotage locals during clan negotiations. Secure the place against remote charges. The device is disguised as a wooden crate. Go to the village, place it in the attic, return.
Alright, now I get it. You work for Duty?
No, I don’t. You won’t know who I work for until I choose. For now, I just want to protect you from a very skilled spider, disguised as a Freedom member here.
Even so — I don’t care. My business is Clear Sky. The rest doesn’t concern me. Well, now it does — the info you’re willing to share.
As you wish. But remember, later may be too late.
Noted, farewell…

I completed your task. The signal jammer is installed. Right after, a group of mercenaries attacked me. I neutralized the Blues, and it worries me. I don’t want to make the syndicate my enemy. So who did I actually help? Who are you, Chekh?

You did help, even without knowing who. I can assure you — no harm comes from it. Consider it a good deed, protecting good people from trouble. Mercenaries attacked? If you killed them, no traces remain — I’ll take care of that.
Alright, but let’s talk about the cost of this good deed. And your calmness surprises me. Don’t you see the syndicate as a serious threat?
You didn’t see Duty as a problem when killing those recon units, did you? Forget the mercenaries. About your payment — what have you heard about the Sin faction?
Nothing much. A clan of fanatic maniacs in the Dark Valley area. Mix of bandits and Monolith. What does that have to do with this?

Outdated info, my friend. The Sin clan everyone knew no longer exists. Survivors are under powerful mentors. Reorganized, joined by top specialists. Experts in reconnaissance and sabotage. Sin now is a group of elite undercover agents across the Zone. Smart, cunning, ruthless, deadly. Some may have extraordinary psi-abilities. No base, no insignias. Can be among Duty, Freedom, or loners. Secretly funded, influential. Conduct covert ops, pit clans against each other, intercept artifact shipments, block arms supply. Pros. Few know them, and those who do stay quiet. One agent may currently be here undercover as a Duty or Freedom member. Sin emissaries recruit experienced stalkers and agents from other factions expertly.
That’s quite info. Useful, but not really relevant to my interests or work.
Noted, but it doesn’t match my current interests.
Not sure this info helps me, but thanks anyway.

Premature conclusions, my friend. Anyway, I warned you. Don’t become their next victim. Who knows where your investigation leads or who stands in your way. But you can rely on me. Also, help the local radio operator and hunter if needed — they’re good people.
Noted. Hopefully, I won’t need your help.
I completed your task. The signal blocker is installed. Right after, a group of controlled mutants attacked me. I doubt that’s a coincidence. So who did I actually help? Who are you, Chekh?

You did help, even without knowing who. I can assure you — no harm comes from it. Consider it a good deed, protecting good people from trouble. Mutants attacked you? And why do you think they were controlled?
Just seemed that way, and I rarely make mistakes. Now, let’s talk about the cost of this good deed. Don’t you see the psionic or controller as a serious threat?

You didn’t see Duty as a problem when killing their recon squads, did you? The psionic, if it’s really him, is a problem — but we’ll get to that. Now, about your payment. What have you heard about the Sin faction?
Nothing special. A clan of fanatic maniacs in the Dark Valley area. Mix of bandits and Monolith. How is that relevant?

Outdated info, my friend. The Sin clan everyone knew no longer exists. Survivors were taken under the wing of very influential people. Reorganized, joined by top specialists from the mainland. Experts in reconnaissance and sabotage. Sin now is a group of elite undercover agents across the Zone. Smart, cunning, ruthless, deadly. Some may have extraordinary psi-abilities. No base, no insignias. Can be among Duty, Freedom, or loners. Powerful backers, strong financing, growing influence. Conduct covert ops, pit clans against each other, intercept artifact shipments, block weapons supply. Pros. Few know them, those who do keep quiet. One agent may currently be here undercover as a Duty or Freedom member. Sin emissaries recruit experienced stalkers and agents from other factions masterfully.
That’s quite a lot. Useful knowledge, but not really relevant to my interests or work.
Noted, but this info doesn’t match my current interests.
Not sure this info will help me in my work, but thanks anyway.

Premature conclusions, my friend. Anyway, I warned you. Don’t become their next victim. Who knows where your investigation leads or who stands in your way. But you can rely on me. Also, help the local radio operator and hunter if needed — they seem solid and you never know what might happen.
Noted. Hopefully, I won’t need your help.
My friend, I need help. A sip of water, if you please. I’ll pay you later.

Looks like you’ve been through a lot — begging for a sip of water. Alright, we’re not beasts and we understand. Here, take the flask. Forget about payment. Consider it a goodwill gesture for future deals.
And they will happen soon, my friend. I won’t forget your generosity.
Listen, friend. What other work can you offer me? Surely there are unresolved problems here.
Yes, there is one big problem, maybe more. The job is risky, and it must be done at night, which increases the danger. Come around three at night and we’ll sort it out.
Alright. I’ll be there at the designated time.
Time seems suitable. I’m ready to hear the details. What’s the matter?

We have uninvited nocturnal creatures — extremely dangerous and deadly. Several scavenger groups have perished. One hunter tracked them and reported them as chimeras. We need urgent action. Hunters are few, so I’d be glad if you help. You proved capable.
I’ll pass for now. Chimera is too dangerous. Hard to handle alone.
Alright, I agree. But I need preparation. Chimeras are no joke. I once killed one in Yantra. Luck mattered more than skill.
No time to prepare. Take a strong gun and plenty of grenades. My man is already waiting. He tracked a creature near the farm. Go there ASAP.
So I’ll have a partner?
Your man? What do you mean?

You’ll work with a partner. No discussion. Yakut — experienced hunter. Together you have a chance to kill it and survive. Or do you need an example of who they’ve eaten? Enough. Go now. Don’t risk the hunter too.
Understood. We’ll handle it as best we can.
I’m from Zmeelov. So we’ll work together?
Looks like it. Couldn’t that dumb bastard find someone better than you?
Apparently not. What now? Did you track the creature?
One tried before. I didn’t want to follow them to the afterlife. I observed. Two chimeras here. One is wounded, probably shot by our colleague before dying. Second is young.
Two creatures. This complicates things. How do we proceed?
Still two. And that’s a big problem. What’s our plan then?
First, secure the farm. I spotted the chimera in the forest behind it. We’ll handle it from there.
Alright.
We’re here. What’s next?
We’ll split up. You handle the wounded one, I’ll deal with the other. Plan’s not perfect, but we have no choice.
Are you sure there were only two?
I hope you were sure there were only two.
Yes, absolutely. Don’t worry about these creatures. If you’ve dealt with them before, you’ll manage. Something else concerns me.
What? Don’t beat around the bush. Did you discover anything while observing the chimeras?
Not your concern. Your task is the chimera. Saw a strange figure in a cloak, didn’t seem fully human. Anyway, deal with your chimera and return. Got it?
Yes, understood. Good luck.
Zmeelov, I killed the chimera. But there were complications.
What? What complications? Couldn’t you handle those nocturnal predators? Where’s Yakut?
He disappeared. How did you know there were multiple chimeras?

I’m asking the questions here. What do you mean disappeared? Are you insane? All competent hunters in the last two days have died. And you’re standing here trying to act all casual? Tell me what happened.

Yakut determined there were two chimeras. One was wounded. He suggested splitting up. I was to deal with the wounded one, Yakut took the healthy one. I handled my creature and returned to the meeting spot. Yakut wasn’t there. No sounds of shooting, fighting, or anything. No trace of the second chimera either. Whether it existed is only Yakut’s assumption. I didn’t risk searching alone at night and returned to base. Yakut disappeared — fact. Alive or dead, I won’t tell. But I completed my task. The chimera is dead.

The chimera is dead, that’s good. Consider the job done. But Yakut’s disappearance or death is concerning. The clan is losing experienced hunters, and we can’t replace them. We’ll have to plead with superiors to transfer some duties to scouts. But that’s not the scariest part. The unknown worries me. Yakut couldn’t just die to some stinking chimera. Even if it was there, he would have handled it. So it was something else. Did you notice anything suspicious?
Yakut mentioned a cloaked figure he saw in the area, but it didn’t look like a controller.
Yakut said something about a person in a cloak. And he wasn’t a controller.

A cloaked figure, you say? There’s a cemetery nearby. Not a friendly one. Who knows what horrors have risen from those graves. Compared to that, chimeras are harmless. But that’s just one theory. Alright, Varg. You did your job, now let the others work. Scouts will sweep the area. Farewell.
Farewell.
Hello. Your leadership said you can be trusted, and I can even approach you for extra work. Is that right?
Yes, that’s correct. Listen, Varg. It may sound silly, but believe me — I had nothing to do with the attack on you. I have no idea how they found out you’d be in that area.
Alright, forget about that attack. If your leadership vouches for you, then so be it. Now, what work can you offer me?

Heard about artifact selection? If not, briefly: it changes their properties from negative to positive, or enhances existing traits. Now the task: I have several artifact samples for you to perform selection near an anomalous field. Not hard, but risky. High chance of getting fried. Will you take it?
No, I’ll pass. Don’t feel like diving into anomalies again.
Alright, I agree. Now more details. Which anomalous field will I enter?
During your previous mission, you collected anomaly scanners. Analyzing results led to important conclusions. Remember the malachite spherical formations in the forest?
Yes, I remember those anomalies.
Of course I remember. First time seeing such things here.

Good. These anomalies have a strange field ideal for selection. Each of the three has a unique field. Here are three artifact samples and special equipment. Go to each anomaly and perform selection on one artifact in its field. Return when done. Clear?
Understood. I’ll do it.
Task complete. Selection performed on all three artifact samples.
Good. Well done handling this task. Give me the artifact samples and equipment.
Here. Did you doubt I could handle it?
Not really. I doubted how dangerous the anomalies might be while you were inside. These malachite formations are little studied.
So I was a guinea pig as well?
You used me as a test subject?

I didn’t say that. You drew that conclusion yourself. Either way, you completed the task. We’ll study the artifacts, and if selection samples have better traits, their value — and the fee for field access — will increase greatly. But that’s another story. You got your share. Farewell.
Alright. Farewell.
Hello. Why do you need me? What happened?
Remember helping me set up Credo radiolocators? One detected something unusual. We sent a scout team there, but they went silent.
Oh no, my friend. Sorry, but I won’t run around finding out what happened to your scouts. Handle it yourselves. I helped place the systems, that’s enough.
What exactly did the radiolocator detect?
Hard to say. Something large, but not a mutant. Too static. Probably a new anomaly. I might be wrong. Adjustments to these radiolocators are ongoing. I changed a lot since you last worked with them.
Let’s skip the technical details this time. Where did this happen?
Be specific. Where exactly?
Not far from the village. The team got there quickly, and we lost contact immediately.
Could it be a strong anomalous field blocking their radios?

Anomalous field — don’t joke. Otherwise, radiolocators wouldn’t detect anything. They clearly spot a large object and several alive humans nearby. Radios are on, but no response. Investigate, carefully.
Alright, I’ll check it out.
What? What happened? Damn, how did I end up here?

You got incredibly lucky. You’re tough, I’ll give you that. The second scout team sent after the missing ones found you barely alive. I managed to stabilize you, but your nervous system may not survive such a shock again. You were hit by a massive psychic strike. That’s the short version. What do you remember?

I remember arriving at the place the radio operator indicated. There was a bloody anomaly. People around it, like in a trance… Then a strike, another strike. They started shooting, I returned fire, then nothing.

Those scouts were far less lucky than you. Their minds were completely destroyed by the psychic effect of an unknown anomaly. Not even sure it was truly an anomaly. Though, let’s say it’s rare. You probably haven’t heard of it yet, but soon everyone will. Go see our radio operator once you recover. He’ll explain the details. After all, it was his idea to send you basically to your death, so let him deal with it.
Yes, you’re right. I’ll go. He’ll explain in detail what I faced. Thanks for saving my life.
So what the hell was that? Trying to get me killed?

I’m as shocked as you are, Varg. Understand, this is the first time we’ve encountered something like this here. You surviving is a miracle, agreed. But you did survive. The scout team was lost — that’s the real tragedy. Harold was furious when he heard.
No wonder he was furious. What kind of anomaly was this? Why wasn’t this known? Or was it? If yes, why didn’t you warn?
Little is known. Here on our territory, it appeared for the first time. It was the “Black Angel.” Not fully, just its embryonic state. Heard of it?

No, never. Psychic strikes, that was its effect? It felt like the anomaly worked like a controller. It destroyed the scouts from inside. They attacked me, I had to kill them all. Then the strike, and I woke up only at the doctor’s.

I know what happened on the road. Fellow stalkers who brought you here explained the details. You were doubly lucky they didn’t kill you themselves. As for the psychic strikes, that’s the mystery. The “Black Angel,” or rather its embryonic state, can’t produce such effects. Some of the most experienced runners believe the anomaly was just a cover.
So you’re saying it was the work of a powerful psionic who controlled the minds of experienced stalkers? And did he create the anomaly with his mind too?

It’s possible. The “Black Angel” anomaly is real. But it’s gone now. It was unstable and couldn’t progress into the next state as an amethyst vortex. Who applied the psychic influence under its cover is a mystery.
And what will you do? A powerful psionic with unclear motives attacks our stalkers. You won’t just leave it?
What are your actions in this situation? You won’t just leave it? The death of the scout team due to a psionic isn’t a joke.

We won’t. But you won’t intervene either. We already owe you too much for everything you’ve done. So this time, we’ll handle it ourselves. I also tweaked the radiolocator settings. I’ll upload something to your PDA. Consider it payment for your risk. These settings let you monitor our systems. You’ll see stalker squads and some mutants. It’s still in test mode, but working. Only our clan stalkers and you will have access. This will really help us stay alive.
If I’m not to interfere, then I won’t. As you say. Thanks for the bonus of a working radiolocation system — it’ll help avoid mutants when encounters are undesirable. Farewell.
So, what work can you offer and why at night specifically?
Hey. Work is work. Nothing special. Low risk, but it must be done. I need help testing night vision devices. Will you take it?
Maybe later, I’ll take it.
Not interested. Won’t take it.
Yes, of course. What exactly will the testing involve?
How will the testing procedure work exactly?

Here’s how. You’ll work with a local stalker, my second assistant. He’ll measure some parameters, you’ll test the devices. Three devices: “Contour,” “Dedal,” maybe you’ve heard of them. Test each, your partner records results, you return. That’s it. Clear?
Task is clear. How will the results affect anything? Planned purchase of a batch of gear again?
Preliminary plans only. Likely only one batch for our scouts. Others can order individually. Borders are restless now. Supplying quality gear is more difficult than before.
Got it. I’ll go then. Will ask you more later.
Hey. I’m from the technician. About testing night vision devices. He said we’ll work together.
Greetings. Yes, we’ll work together. Your task: start the device, stay close, test it, I’ll record the necessary data. Got it? Here’s the first sample.
Yes, got it. How exactly will you record the data?
God knows. I’m just helping like you. The technician gave the gear, explained buttons, the rest is up to him.
Fair enough.
All readings taken?
Yes. Here’s the second device. Do the same. This one seems slightly better.
Looks like it. Quality improves progressively. Haha, they’ll buy the cheapest crap anyway. What about the first one?

Return all devices to the technician at base. As for purchases, maybe you’re right, but quality equipment is bought here if needed. Serious business. Leadership handles money you wouldn’t imagine and wants the business to thrive.
Let’s hope you’re right.
Last device. Let’s finish and leave. Wonder how much Tokar will pay for this?
Who knows. The work is trivial, but Tokar seems fair, he won’t cheat.
True. How long have you been in their clan?

About four months. Getting used to the place. Relatively quiet. Mutants, yes — many, but other group stalkers are rare. That’s nice. At the dump, things are getting messy. Interests of Duty, Freedom, rogues, and bandits collide. Could turn into a serious fight.
True, but other factions have interests here too. But that’s another story.
So, my friend. The work is done, all readings are taken. My partner should have given you the results himself — he didn’t say anything?
Don’t worry about him. Payment is separate — each for their own work. Yours for yours, his for his. Hand me the samples, I’ll calculate your share.
Here you go. By the way, wouldn’t it have been easier for you to run these tests yourself? At least cheaper.
Take it. By the way — wouldn’t it have been easier for you to conduct these tests yourself?
And who would repair equipment and gear while doing that — you? Exactly. That’s why I offer people various jobs they can handle while I focus on my main duties here.
Got it. You mentioned recent problems with gear supply. Is it serious? What could be causing it?

Hardly serious. Everyone’s sitting on a gold—or rather platinum—vein here. Military, stalkers, traders, middlemen — all profit from artifact sales. Officially, the Zone is dangerous and off-limits. In reality, it’s serious business run by serious people. Supply issues arise when either the military does something for show or someone disagrees about kickbacks. That’s about it.
Understood. Farewell.
Hello. Why did you call me? What exactly needs to be done?
Specifically — we need to settle the issue with Hasan once and for all. This man has done too much, it’s time to remove him.
And I suppose I’m the one who has to deal with him?
Exactly. It happens that you’re the best candidate for this task. Sorry to put you on a dirty job, but this bastard deserves no mercy. Plus, it needs to be done quietly.
Why such complications? Wouldn’t it be simpler to handle him directly, especially with the solid evidence you have?

He may still have loyal people in his faction. Not so much loyal as simply obedient. Even though Hasan is effectively a mercenary serving both his interests and those of his patrons. The clan’s stalkers might not accept us just shooting him in front of the base. So it must be done quietly and peacefully. There are already a few options for this.
And what are the options?
What are the options?

Well, you see. You could set up an ambush — lure him into the woods under some false pretext and deal with him there. The radio operator can fake a transmission, no problem. If you go this route, tell him it’s my personal order. Or deal with him here at the base. The second option is trickier — essentially, we do to him what he did to the scouts. I’ll ensure there’s a catalyst in his building, and you plant a chemically-triggered charge. No one will be hurt except him. So, choose wisely. The quartermaster will help with supplies and notify me if you go for the second method.
Understood. I’ll decide what’s best.
Hello. I need your help with a rather delicate matter.
Depends how delicate.

Very delicate. I need you to inform Hasan that a group of collectors reportedly found a rare artifact worth a fortune but fell into a bandit ambush and need urgent help. Make it convincing. The goal is to get Hasan to personally go to that area.
Are you serious? Do you realize what you’re asking me?
Absolutely. This is Harold’s direct order. I’m executing it for him. Make sure Hasan moves immediately to that area.
Understood. What about yourself?
Delivering justice, my friend... Delivering justice.
Hasan is dead. I did everything necessary. Worked through the radio operator. Hasan and his men at the designated point — all eliminated.
Great, excellent work. Now we can stabilize the clan without that bastard.
What about his remaining loyal people? I asked about this when we discovered the technician’s involvement.
The obedient ones will find out and move on — they’re not dangerous. Those closely connected who remain alive — we’ll surveil them. If they cause trouble, same fate as Hasan.
Agreed. Wise approach.
Wise decision.

Exactly. Now about you. You’ve proven your worth to our faction. I understand you’re here for your own goals, but I don’t want to lose someone valuable like you. I propose you take Hasan’s place by my side. The only requirement — loyalty. You’ve seen what happens to those who betray me. Those who follow me rise to great heights. With your mind and foresight, we’ll strengthen our influence and earn a fortune.
Thanks for the trust. I’ll consider your offer. For now — I must settle my own affairs.
Hey. I need your help with a task.
Speak plainly — what task?
I work on Harold’s order. I need an explosive charge with a special chemical filler. I think you know what I mean?
Yes, I understand. So Hasan was indeed behind much of the recent chaos here?
Not everything, but mostly he was involved. And all questions go to Harold. I just do my job, you do yours.
Understood. I’ll tell Harold you’ll handle the chemical mine. Do it cleanly and carefully. The charge is set so no one gets hurt except Hasan.
Everything will be as needed. Don’t worry.
Don’t worry. Everything will be perfect.
Hasan is dead. I did everything necessary. Worked through the quartermaster. Hasan was eliminated using the poison gas, as you advised.
Great, excellent work. Now we can stabilize the clan without that bastard.
What about his remaining loyal people? I asked about this when we discovered the technician’s involvement.
The obedient ones will hear of his death and move on. They’re not dangerous. Those closely connected who are still alive — we’ll surveil them. If they cause trouble, same fate as Hasan.
Agreed. Wise move.
Wise decision.

Exactly. Now about you. You’ve proven your value to our faction. I understand you’re here for your own goals, but I don’t want to lose someone valuable like you. Therefore, I propose you take Hasan’s place by my side. The only thing I’ll need is loyalty. You’ve seen what happens to those who betray me. Those who follow me will rise to unparalleled heights. With your mind and foresight, we’ll strengthen our influence and earn a fortune.
Thank you for your trust. I’ll consider your offer. For now — I must take care of my own affairs.
Hello. Why did you call me? What exactly needs to be done?

Specifically — we need to settle the issue with Harold once and for all. It’s time to act more decisively and take power into our own hands. Harold has outlived his usefulness. He’s a thing of the past.
And I suppose I’m the one who has to deal with him?
Exactly. It happens that you’re the best candidate for this task. Sorry to put you on a dirty job, but circumstances turned out this way. Plus, it must be done quietly.
Why such complications? Wouldn’t it be simpler to handle him directly? Especially since most in the clan also want change and would openly support us.

Harold’s influence is still too strong. An open revolt could cause unnecessary casualties. Most wouldn’t accept us just walking in and shooting him in front of the base. So it must be done quietly and peacefully. There are no other candidates for leadership — power will naturally come to me. Removing him is both wise and merciful. There are already a few possible ways to accomplish this.
And what are the options?
What options do we have?

Look. You could set up an ambush — lure him into the woods under some false pretext and deal with him there. The radio operator can fake a transmission — no problem. If you go this way, tell him it’s my personal order. Or handle him directly at the base. The second option is trickier — essentially, do to him what we did with the Lame group. I’ll ensure a catalyst is in his building, and you plant a chemically-triggered charge. No one will be hurt except him. Choose wisely. The quartermaster will assist with supplies and notify me if you go for the second method. Remember — in this case, the quartermaster will help, not the technician.
Understood. I’ll decide what’s best.
Hello. I need your help with a rather delicate matter.
Depends how delicate.

Very delicate. I need you to tell Harold that a collector group reportedly found a rare artifact worth a fortune but fell into a bandit ambush and need urgent help. Make it convincing. The goal is to get Harold to personally go to that area.
Are you serious? Do you realize what you’re asking me?

Absolutely. This is Hasan’s direct order. I’m working for him now. He has undeniable proof that Harold himself was involved in attacks on collectors and connections with mercenaries. He just used everyone for his own gain. Make sure Harold moves immediately to that area.
Understood. What about yourself?
Delivering justice, my friend... Delivering justice.
Harold is dead. I did everything necessary. Worked through the radio operator. The former clan leader was at the designated point with his men — all eliminated.
Great, excellent work. Now we can start the clan’s new life without this conservative.
What about his remaining loyal people?
The obedient ones will hear of his death and move on. They’re not dangerous. Those closely connected who are still alive — we’ll surveil them. If they cause trouble, same fate as Harold.
Agreed. That’s the right decision.
That’s the correct approach.

Now about you. You’ve proven both your resilience and your usefulness. First, forget all previous misunderstandings between us. Second — I propose you take an important role in the clan as an operative. The only requirement is your loyalty. With your mind and foresight, we’ll strengthen our influence in this difficult business.
Thank you for your trust. I’ll consider your offer. For now — I must take care of my own affairs.
Hey. I need your help with a task.
Speak plainly — what task?
I work on Hasan’s order. I need an explosive charge with a special chemical filler. I think you know what I mean?
Yes, I understand. So Harold was truly behind much of the recent chaos here?
Not everything, but mostly he was personally involved. And all questions go to Hasan. I just do my job, you do yours.
Understood. I’ll tell Hasan you handled the second method. Do it carefully and cleanly. The charge is set so no one is hurt except Harold himself.
Everything will be as needed. Don’t worry.
Everything will be done perfectly.
Harold is dead. I did everything necessary. Worked through the quartermaster. The former leader was eliminated using poison gas, as you advised.
Great, excellent work. Now we can start the clan’s new life without this conservative.
What about his remaining loyal people?
The obedient ones will hear of his death and move on. They’re not dangerous. Those closely connected who are still alive — we’ll surveil them. If they cause trouble, same fate as Harold.
Agreed. That’s the right decision.

Now about you. You’ve proven both your resilience and your usefulness. First, forget all previous misunderstandings — that’s one. Second, I propose you take an important role in the clan as an operative. The only thing I’ll need is your loyalty. With your mind and foresight, we’ll strengthen our influence in this difficult business.
Thank you for your trust. I’ll consider your offer. For now — I must take care of my own affairs.
By the way, don’t you have any more work to offer?

There are a couple of jobs. They’re not urgent, but it’s better to do them and forget. Quite risky, but surely you’ve encountered similar tasks during your stalker career. This is about retrieving rare artifacts on request. Currently, the faction has two unfulfilled contracts from the scientists in Yantra. First — to get the Black Soul artifact, second — to find the Dragon’s Eye. The artifacts are very rare, but we’ve learned to predict their possible appearances in certain anomalies. So the search area will be narrowed. If you’re interested, you can take one of these tasks. But keep in mind that Black Soul is dangerous. Without proper transport, it can harm humans. Of course, the payment will be higher.
Maybe I’ll wait on artifact retrieval for now.
I’ll take the Dragon’s Eye. I don’t have this artifact yet.
I’ll take on the search for the Black Soul. I’ve encountered it once before.
Wise choice. Dragon’s Eye doesn’t have the harmful effect of Black Soul. And it will count in your personal achievements.
And it saves you effort. By the way, where should I look for it?
Plus, it will save you effort. Which area will the search cover?
Can’t tell. Someone will have to fulfill the second contract anyway. There are plenty of people wanting to make money. Search area — south, near the checkpoint.
Okay, I’ll do it.
I hope you’ve thought this through and really know what you’re getting into. In pursuit of big money, many stalkers lost their souls.
Don’t worry, I know. By the way, which area should I search?
The search area — near the farm. It’s quite remote and dangerous; the cursed cemetery is nearby. So be doubly careful.
Don’t worry, I’ll do everything properly. Prepare my share.
Approach slowly and don’t even think about drawing your gun. So, punk, did you really think we wouldn’t find you?
What are you talking about? I’m here on my own business, didn’t cross your way. Mistaken for someone else?
Don’t play dumb, Freeman scum. Remember the checkpoint? Our scouts you took out? Did you really think the zone’s most powerful clan would forget that?
I recall. They were in the wrong place at the wrong time, and mistook me for someone else. Just like you now.
Doesn’t matter. We always settle debts. More than fully.
And what are you planning to do...
Hey punk, approach slowly and don’t even think about drawing your gun. So, brat, did you think we wouldn’t find you?
What are you talking about, Freedom junkie? I’m here on my own business, didn’t cross your way. Mistaken for someone?
Don’t play dumb, Dolg scum. Remember the checkpoint? Our scouts you took out? Did you really think the zone’s most powerful clan would forget that?
I recall. They were in the wrong place at the wrong time. And I didn’t take them out — your Dolg friends did. But I did take care of the others — my work.
And you think we’ll believe that crap? Maybe we should give you a medal too? Although… maybe not.
And what are you planning to do...
Hasan, I urgently need your help. Can you hear me out?
Speak, since you started, but quickly. I don’t have time to waste on nonsense.
It won’t take long. In short — I have serious conflicts with the Freedom faction and need support to resolve this.
Why should I wipe your tears and risk the clan’s reputation protecting an enemy of Freedom? Freemen are cruel and powerful. I have no desire to quarrel for some vagabond.

What if I tell you these Freemen will threaten your clan’s interests — would that change your response? I’m not just some vagabond. You probably remember the Freemen and Dolg squad was recently wiped out at your border. Everyone thought it was mutual, but it wasn’t. I was there and played a direct role.
Go on...
The Freemen just discovered me and declared a hunt. I survived their brutal game, but they won’t stop. The deceased Freemen and Dolg planned to start sabotage against your clan...

Don’t exaggerate. I don’t think Dolg and Freedom came here in sync just to profit. But they are unwanted guests. Alright, stalker, consider yourself heard. I’ll probably help you, and myself mostly. Come to me at night. We’ll find a solution. Consider this payment for future work. After their game, you’ll need to recover some gear. Now — leave.
Thank you.
It won’t take long. In short — I have serious conflicts with the Dolg faction and need support to resolve this.
Why should I wipe your tears and risk the clan’s reputation protecting a Dolg enemy? They are cruel and powerful. I have no desire to quarrel for some vagabond.

What if I tell you these Dolg will threaten your clan’s interests — would that change our conversation? I’m not just a vagabond. You probably remember the Freemen and Dolg squad was recently wiped out at your border. Everyone thought it was mutual, but it wasn’t. I was there and involved directly.
Go on...
The Dolg just discovered me and declared a hunt. I survived, but they won’t stop. The deceased Freemen and Dolg planned sabotage against your clan...

Don’t exaggerate. I don’t think Dolg and Freedom came here in sync just to profit. But they are unwanted guests. Alright, stalker, consider yourself heard. I’ll probably help you, and myself mostly. Come to me at night. We’ll find a solution. Consider this payment for future work. After their game, you’ll need to recover some gear. Now — leave.
Thank you.
Harold, I need your help. Urgently. Can you hear me out?
Greetings. Speak, since you’ve started. I hope this is something serious if you came to me directly.
This won’t take long. In short — I’ve run into serious conflicts with the Freedom faction and need support to resolve it.

Why would you think I’d wipe your nose for you and risk the clan’s reputation covering an enemy of Freedom? The Freemen are a ruthless and powerful group. I have no desire to quarrel with them over some simple stalker. Sorry, but the interests of the clan come first for me.

What if I told you that these very Freedom fighters could threaten your clan’s interests — would that steer our conversation in the right direction? Besides, I’m not just any simple stalker. Surely you remember the Freemen and Duty squads that were recently wiped out on the border of your territories. Everyone assumed they killed each other. That’s not true. I was there personally involved.

I see. So you’re the Rambo who wiped out squads of Duty and Freedom. Naturally, they were pissed and came after you. And now you crawl to me, whining and tail tucked, asking for help. Right? I’d gladly hand you over to them myself, but I understand as well as you that their presence here wasn’t accidental and certainly not because of you. So, I think we can work something out.

The Freemen only just tracked me down and declared a hunt. I survived their brutal game, but I’m sure they won’t stop. The fallen Freedom and Duty members had indeed planned to sabotage your clan, and now all evidence of my words is in their hands.

Careful, stalker, don’t overdo it. I don’t think Duty and Freedom came here in perfect sync just for easy profit. But they are definitely uninvited guests and clearly out of place in this little party of life. Alright, consider yourself heard. I’ll probably help you, and the clan as well — mostly the clan. Come to me at night. We’ll find a solution to your problem. And by the way — consider this payment for future work. After their game, you’ll need to restore some of your gear. Now — go.
Thank you.
This won’t take long. In short — I’ve run into serious conflicts with the Duty faction and need support to resolve it.

Why would you think I’d wipe your nose for you and risk the clan’s reputation covering an enemy of Duty? They’re a ruthless and powerful faction. I have no desire to quarrel with them over some simple stalker. Sorry, but the clan’s interests come first for me.

What if I told you that these Duty fighters could threaten your clan’s interests — would that change the course of our conversation? Besides, I’m not just any simple stalker. Surely you remember the Freemen and Duty squads that were recently wiped out on the border of your territories. Everyone assumed they killed each other. That’s not true. I was there personally involved.

I see. So you’re the Rambo who wiped out squads of Duty and Freedom. Naturally, they were pissed and came after you. And now you crawl to me, whining and tail tucked, asking for help. Right? I’d gladly hand you over to them myself, but I understand as well as you that their presence here wasn’t accidental and certainly not because of you. So, I think we can work something out.

The Duty fighters only just tracked me down and declared a hunt. I survived their brutal game, but I’m sure they won’t stop. The fallen Freedom and Duty members had indeed planned to sabotage your clan, and now all evidence of my words is in their hands.

Careful, stalker, don’t overdo it. I don’t think Duty and Freedom came here in perfect sync just for easy profit. But they are definitely uninvited guests and clearly out of place in this little party of life. Alright, consider yourself heard. I’ll probably help you, and the clan as well — mostly the clan. Come to me at night. We’ll find a solution to your problem. And by the way — consider this payment for future work. After their game, you’ll need to restore some of your gear. Now — go.
Thank you.
Well, looks like the time is right. So, what’s the plan? What exactly are you thinking?

Don’t rush. You’ll know soon enough. My people double-checked everything, compared the facts with your words. A few questions came up. How exactly did you eliminate those Duty and Freedom scout groups? And most importantly — why? Think carefully before answering.

Here’s what happened. I ended up on your territory, and the first people I ran into were a Freedom patrol. As it turned out, it wasn’t even a patrol, but part of their recon squad. They offered me a small job — I agreed. When I came back, the Freedom squad had already been wiped out. The Duty fighters tracked and eliminated them. They mistook me for another Freedom member and opened fire. I returned fire. And I got lucky…
I see. So in fact, you only took out the Duty fighters. Interesting. Noted.
Yes, essentially. Though the Freedom hunters just opened season on me. So, what now?

Simple. We kill those in your way. More precisely, you will. My people arranged for your enemies to occupy one of our temporary checkpoints. You go there and eliminate everyone present. Then you come back to me, and we’ll discuss the proper cover story. The rest will be handled by others. Remember — my primary loyalty is to the clan. I don’t need roaming scouts from other factions here.
Alright, I’ll take care of it.
Yes, essentially. And the Duty fighters have opened season on me now. So, what do we do now?

Simple. We kill those in your way. More precisely, you will. My people arranged for your enemies to occupy one of our temporary checkpoints. You go there and eliminate everyone present. Then you return to me, and we’ll discuss the proper cover story. The rest will be handled by others. Remember — my primary loyalty is to the clan. I don’t need roaming scouts from other factions here.
Alright, I’ll take care of it.
Hasan, everything that depended on me — has been successfully done.
Excellent. I hope that now both my problem and yours will be resolved. The guys are finishing up covering the tracks the way we need.
And how exactly do you need it? You also mentioned a follow-up story.

We’ll skip the fine details. I’ll tell you only what you need to know. Here’s the deal. Through my channels, I arranged with the Duty fighters to smooth over the possible conflict. Remember this about the first day, when both Duty and Freedom squads were killed: you were not at that checkpoint, you didn’t see the Freedom fighters and you didn’t work for them. You didn’t witness the Duty fighters arriving and killing their enemies. You appeared there later with my scouts and only found the bodies left behind. The attack by the Duty squad on you was a mistake — they thought you were a Freedom agent, but I convinced their leadership otherwise. Got it? Forget this story and never mention it again if you want to stay alive a little longer. And about your elimination of the second Duty squad — don’t bother thanking me, but if you ever face a critical choice, remember that Hasan once saved your ass. Now, disappear from sight.
I understand. Thanks anyway. Goodbye.
And how exactly do you need it? You also mentioned a follow-up story.

We’ll skip the fine details. I’ll tell you only what you need to know. Here’s the deal. Through my channels, I arranged with the Freedom fighters to smooth over the possible conflict. Remember this about the first day, when both Duty and Freedom squads were killed: you were not at that checkpoint, you didn’t see the Freedom fighters and you didn’t work for them. You didn’t witness the Duty fighters arriving and killing their enemies. You appeared there later with my scouts and only found the bodies left behind. The attack by the Freedom squad on you was a mistake — they thought you were a Duty agent, but I convinced their leadership otherwise. Got it? Forget this story and never mention it again if you want to stay alive a little longer. And about your elimination of the second Freedom squad — don’t bother thanking me, but if you ever face a critical choice, remember that Hasan once saved your ass. Now, disappear from sight.
I understand. Thanks anyway. Goodbye.
Well, seems like the time is right. So, what’s the plan? What exactly have you decided to do?
