Don’t rush. You’ll find out soon enough. My people double-checked everything, comparing facts with your words. A few questions came up. How exactly did you eliminate those Duty and Freedom scout groups? And most importantly — why? Think carefully before answering.

Here’s what happened. I ended up on your territory, and the first people I ran into were a Freedom patrol. As it turned out, it wasn’t even a patrol, but part of their recon squad. They offered me a small job — I agreed. On my way back, the Freedom squad had already been wiped out. The Duty fighters tracked and eliminated them. They mistook me for another Freedom member and opened fire. I returned fire. And I got lucky...
I see. So, in fact, you only took out the Duty fighters. Interesting information. That changes a lot, but don’t worry — it’s manageable.
Yes, essentially. Though the Freedom hunters just opened season on me. So, what now?

Simple. We kill those in your way. More precisely, you will. My people arranged for your enemies to occupy one of our temporary checkpoints. You go there and eliminate everyone present. Then you return to me, and we’ll discuss the proper cover story. The rest will be handled by others. Remember — I help my clan first. I don’t need roaming scouts from other factions here.
Alright, I’ll take care of it.
Yes, essentially. And the Duty fighters have opened season on me now. So, what do we do now?

Simple. We kill those in your way. More precisely, you will. My people arranged for your enemies to occupy one of our temporary checkpoints. You go there and eliminate everyone present. Then return to me, and we’ll discuss the proper cover story. The rest will be handled by others. Remember — I help my clan first. I don’t need roaming scouts from other factions here.
Alright, I’ll take care of it.
Harold, everything that depended on me — has been successfully done.
Excellent. I hope that now both my problem and yours will be resolved. The guys are finishing up covering the tracks the way we need.
And how exactly do you need it? You also mentioned a follow-up story.

We’ll skip the fine details. I’ll tell you only what you need to know. Here’s the deal. Through my channels, I arranged with the Duty fighters to smooth over the possible conflict. Remember this about the first day, when both Duty and Freedom squads were killed: you were not at that checkpoint, you didn’t see the Freedom fighters and you didn’t work for them. You didn’t witness the Duty fighters arriving and killing their enemies. You appeared there later with my scouts and only found the bodies left behind. The attack by the Duty squad on you was a mistake — they thought you were a Freedom agent, but I convinced their leadership otherwise. Got it? Forget this story and never mention it again if you want to stay alive a little longer. And about your elimination of the second Duty squad — don’t bother thanking me, but if you ever face a critical choice, remember that Harold once saved your ass. Now, disappear from sight.
I understand. Thanks anyway. Goodbye.
Good day. I’m looking for work. Do you need any help? Maybe I could be of use for some task?

And the same to you, stalker. Honestly, everyone’s fully loaded right now — supplies, couriers… you know how it is. It all requires skill and experience. And you’re new here. But I think I have a suitable job for you. Precisely because you’re new, that’s what we’ll use.
Maybe I’ll come back later.
I’m willing to work. But for now, it’s not clear what I’ll actually be doing. Can you be more specific?

Of course. You’ll be carrying out several tasks aimed at benefiting the clan. That’s the official cover for everyone else. Don’t be surprised. In reality, you’ll be primarily involved in an investigation initiated by the clan leader Harold, aimed at identifying artifact collectors who have started working against the faction. Recently, there have been increasing cases of artifacts being sold to outsiders, attacks on certain collector groups, and other sabotage. For example, some faction caches were recently looted. And it’s believed all these events are somehow connected. So we need to figure it out — and, most importantly, we need a new person to handle part of the work.
Sounds like an interesting job. I like it. But tell me, why did you decide that artifact collectors are involved?

They are involved. Rest assured. You’re right that there’s no clear leader among them, but there are definitely link nodes. Perhaps there’s a leader too. Our scouts have already caught and interrogated ordinary bandits. Some clearly acted on orders and had supervisors. But who gives instructions to those supervisors is a big question. Someone from inside is informing them. We need to figure out who. We’ll start with the collectors — or rather, their coordinators. Each collector group has a coordinator who manages several groups, issues current routes, other info, and collects the most valuable artifacts. We have three coordinators. They deliver the best items either to me or to our artifact specialist. We’ll begin by checking these coordinators.
And how exactly?

You’ll become my personal courier. I’ve arranged with Jorah to inform them of your authority. You’ll go to each of them and collect the artifacts they were supposed to deliver. Try to get them talking, act like a naive rookie who can’t keep a secret. Then bring up the recent cache looting and say you’ve been assigned to restore them. Give each collector coordinates of a new cache — I’ll provide them to you now.
So the key is to give each of them slightly different info? And you think they’ll fall for it?
Whether they fall for it or not — I don’t know. But we have to start somewhere. Do what I ask first, then we’ll see if I was right.
Alright.
Hey. I’m the new courier from Isai. He said you’d be informed when I arrived.
Yeah. I was told. Well, there aren’t many artifacts at the moment, but I’ll hand over whatever there is.
Good. By the way, have you heard anything about the looted faction caches? Rumor has it some scavengers found them. Now I’ll have to hide a new one near the abandoned farmstead. What a pain.
No, haven’t heard. I get it, you’ll be running back and forth. But hang in there — everyone’s got their own patch.
True. Noticed anything unusual, by the way?
All quiet so far. Everyone’s completing their tasks. Mutants as usual, countless. Just the usual grind. We’re expecting a shipment of new gear soon, so push Isai a bit.
Will do. Take care.
Hi. I’m the new courier from Isai. He should have informed you.
Yeah. I was told you’d be arriving. I’ll hand over the artifacts collected. Anything else needed? Any additional instructions?
Good. By the way, have you heard about the looted faction caches? Rumor says some bandits found them. Now I’ll have to stash a new one near the central outpost. What a hassle.
No, haven’t heard. The guys haven’t mentioned anything either. I’d know if something like that happened in our area.
Alright. Noticed anything else unusual? Any clues could be important.

Only one odd mutant. Looks like a controller. Our scouts spotted it north of the village. They reported it — now we wait to see what they decide. Pass this to Isai. No telling what this freak really is. A controller would attack, but this one left, which is suspicious.
Will do. Take care.
Hello. I’m the new courier from Isai. You should’ve been informed about my arrival.

Correct — I was told. I’ve prepared everything needed. The guys have some requests too. Deliver them along with the artifacts. Supposedly, new gear shipments were planned a while ago, but Isai’s dragging his feet. The guys are frustrated — no proper binoculars, detectors, or night vision. What a mess. He better stop loafing and get to work.

Alright, I’ll pass your requests to him. By the way, heard anything about the looted faction caches? Rumor says some bandits found them. Now I’ll have to stash a new one near the southern outpost. What a headache.

No, haven’t heard. Not surprising. With slackers like Isai, it’s a miracle anything still holds together. Long overdue to kick that lazy bastard. I wouldn’t be surprised if he drunkenly blabbed where the caches were.
Sure, that’s your opinion — but I haven’t noticed anything like that from him.

Heh, you’re green here, barely a week in. My advice — stop serving Isai. Go to Tokar instead. One of the few worthy people in the clan. Even the leadership respects him. Especially Hasan. Not like this under-supplied fool.
I’ll think about your suggestion. Take care.
I did everything as you told me. Took the artifacts, had a few talks, planted the new cache locations with each of them. Now I think I understand why it was needed.

Glad you got it. We told one collector the cache would be near the farmstead, another in the center, the third near the southern outpost. Now we wait to see which cache gets looted — that’ll reveal the bastard.
Let’s hope they make their move. By the way, one of the collectors or coordinators was rather aggressive.
Who exactly?
Some Mahir. He’s extremely unhappy with your work. Thinks you’re incompetent. The groups have been waiting for new gear for ages but can’t get it. He blames you.
I see. Good to know. Thanks. That’s an important observation. I have an idea now — just need to check if it’s correct. When you’re ready for the next step, come see me. We’ll check the floats.
Alright.
I’m ready to handle the artifact collector Mahir.
Good. Here’s the plan — I consulted leadership and we decided to eliminate him.
What? He’s probably an important link in the chain! His job was just passing tips, surely someone is above him. Shouldn’t he be interrogated?

True, he should. But I warned you not to ask unnecessary questions. Interrogation changes nothing. He’s not the type you can crack. Besides, we already suspect his connections. Confirming that again is pointless.
Don’t get me wrong. It’s foolish to remove a valuable witness just like that. But fine. It’s your mess in the clan to clean up. I don’t really care.

His death will serve as a warning to anyone thinking of betraying the clan. Consider it a message delivered via Mahir’s corpse. Those behind him aren’t fools — they’ll know we’re aware and can act anytime. Don’t overthink it. Deliver this package to Mahir — looks like a simple gun, but it’s no ordinary piece.
Your will. I’ll do what’s needed.
All will be done as required.
Greetings. I’m here from Isai again. This time it’s about you personally.
Really? And he’s sending his little monkey to me again. What’s he lacking? The courage to handle delicate matters himself? When can we expect his damned shipments?
I don’t know what you’re talking about. I don’t care about your relationship with Isai. Here’s the deal — he asked me to give this to you.
And what’s this junk? Why would I need a simple gun? I don’t care about toys, I need proper gear for my guys.
He asks you to forgive him. The equipment shipment is delayed. This gun is a personal gift — also a sample of the quality of weapons that will be given to some collectors later.

Fine, I’ll wait. But my people won’t. Isai or Jorah won’t get valuable artifacts until proper gear arrives. Tell these bastards that. And next time, don’t scare me with threats — I couldn’t care less.
Don’t worry, I’ll pass it on. And I assure you — they won’t threaten you again.

Mahir, I’ve got bad news. Isai decided to take you out. Can’t say more for now, but I don’t agree with him. That’s why I warned you. If you’ve got an alternate route off clan-controlled territory, now’s the time to use it.

So that’s how it is. The little monkey’s getting smarter by the day. And I take it you’re expecting a generous reward? Fine, I hope you’re telling the truth. We’ll head to the northern outpost using a hidden route. If all goes well — I vanish, and I pay you.
Go on, but we need to move fast.
I don’t give a damn. I was ordered to kill you. But I won’t. Isai is wrong. Dead wrong. I’ll save your life and get you out through the southern outpost, but you’ll answer my questions.

What a turn of events. Didn’t expect that. I guess I have no choice — I’ll answer your questions. One thing though — why the southern outpost? Wouldn’t the northern one be safer? I’ve got a better chance of leaving unnoticed there.
Didn’t you get me, damn it? You go through the southern outpost. Argue, and I’ll put you down on the spot. Move.
Hey, kid. Slow and steady, come closer. Reach for your gun… yeah, you know what I mean.
Have you lost it? I just saved your life.
I appreciate your noble act. But your greed for cash backfired on you.
So that’s how you repay someone who helped you?
This is the payback, my friend. You exposed me to Isai. Now I have to flee this place and find a better deal. Consider us square. These temporary troubles I’ll survive, unlike you.
I don’t think so...
Alright, I think we’re here. From here, you’re on your own.
I don’t know why you decided to spare my life, but thanks.
For selfish reasons. Don’t think I’m some saint. Dead men are useless, but a live one can still be of profit. Tell me — you set up the attack on me when I was talking about the caches?

Yes, I set it up. I was ordered to conduct several sabotages within the faction. Loot some caches, organize attacks on collector groups supervised by other coordinators. The actual attackers were just bandits — I only passed along information from one person.
And who is this person? Think carefully, or I might reconsider sparing you.
Who is this person? Think before answering me.

This person is one of the mercenaries, but well-connected with someone high in our clan. I don’t know exactly who. Maybe your Isai — since he decided to eliminate me. Makes sense. I slipped up, and he wants me gone. Who knows now. The main point — someone in the clan has arrangements with the mercenaries. Why the sabotages were needed — I don’t know.
Fine. Get out of here.
Mahir’s dead. There were some complications, but it got done.
Complications, huh? What kind?
He somehow realized I was coming to kill him and resisted. There was a firefight, and he died in it.
Seems he realized they wanted him gone and tried to fight back. He died in the firefight.
Was anyone else with him?
Yes, a few. As far as I know, they were a team of collectors he supervised. Shame. They died needlessly. Foolish to lose your life for others’ sins.

No pity. They surely knew Mahir’s business. But fine, it’s a lesson for the rest. You did well and proved yourself. Keep it up, and you could do well in our clan. Good working with a competent man. Take care.
Take care.
Mahir’s dead. Some complications arose, but it got done.
Complications? What happened?
He somehow guessed I was coming to eliminate him and tried to flee. Threw a grenade at me and bolted. But he didn’t get far — hit a trap, and his guts and bones were scattered around.
He’s dead, that’s what matters. Anyone else with him?
No, he was alone. He expected me where you said. What now?
He was alone. What now?
Nothing now. You did well and proved yourself. Keep it up, and you could do well in our clan. Good working with a competent man. Take care.
Take care.
Mahir’s dead. Everything went smoothly.
No complications at all?
None. I handed him your package and started leaving. A small directional charge went off and sent Mahir to his ancestors.
Good. Was anyone else with him? Any witnesses?
No one. He waited for me exactly where you said. What now?
No one was with him. What now?
Nothing now. You did well and proved yourself. Keep it up, and you could make a good place for yourself in our clan. It was a pleasure working with a competent man. Take care.
Take care.
Hey. I’m looking for work. Can you help me out?
I could. But I won’t. You’re new here, and I don’t know if you’re trustworthy. Talk to our radio guy. Sometimes he gives work to newcomers. Work for him first, then you can come to me.
Understood.
Greetings. I’m interested in work. Got anything for me?
Work, huh? Not with just anyone, my friend. Prove your worth to our group first, then I’ll deal with you.
Alright.
My respects. Listen, friend, I need a favor. I’m looking for work. Can you offer anything?

You’re an outsider, right? Sorry, can’t help yet. Well, maybe I can. But I need to know if I can do business with you. Our artifact specialist usually works with outsiders. If you handle his tasks, you can come to me.
Got it. Thanks.
My respects. I’m new here. Looking for work. Can you help?
Who are you? New? Go bother the collectors and scouts instead. They’ve taken in degenerates... Show yourself first, then come back — then we’ll talk.
On my way.
My respects. Looking for work. I’m new here. Can you help?
New, and you’re coming straight to the clan leader? If you’re not some super-Rambo, sorry — nothing to discuss. Work for the people in the clan first, then come back if you prove yourself.
Understood.
Hey friend. Looking for work. Got anything in mind?
New, and straight to the hunter? Tired of life already? Go help the guys out first. Patrols, for example... Then come back to me.
Alright.
About your offer to join the clan — is it still on?
Yes, why? You’ve settled your business and decided to stay?

Something like that. The Clear Sky problem’s solved and I have nowhere else to go. Paths to Freedom or Duty are closed, even though the conflict there is sorted. Becoming a marauder or a hired gun isn’t for me. I’m an outcast, tossed around. I just want peace.

Outcast, huh? Don’t exaggerate. You haven’t even seen what it means to be truly exiled. I stand by my words — people like you are needed in our clan. I have big plans for expansion. We need to strengthen influence in key areas and consider pushing toward the northern territories.
Promising idea. I could start establishing contacts up north. I’ve been stuck in your swamps too long...

Don’t rush. First, tell me exactly what you’ve been doing here. I know you worked for Clear Sky, but as I understand it, your paths diverged and your last contract caused problems. I need to know — will you bring those problems into the clan? Did you resolve everything? What exactly happened after your meeting with the Clear Sky agent?
As I said — the Clear Sky problem’s resolved. The issue with the stalker scientists too. Everything else doesn’t matter. I’m here and ready to work. Nothing else matters...
Fine. We’ll go with your word. Still, I’ll check what you call “everything else.” You’re free for now. Rest. I’ll call you when the northern expansion plan is ready. Go...
I’ll be waiting. Take care...
Hey. I’m from Chekh. Got a job. You know what I mean?
Uh, from Chekh? Sorry, not really… What exactly do you need from me?
I need you to contact the Clear Sky agent and set up a meeting with him immediately.
Ah, right… Chekh, the Clear Sky agent. Got it. No problem. I’ll handle it. You can head to that area already.
Thanks. By the way, how do you know Chekh?
How do you know Chekh?

From the same place as you. He worked here for a while, then left. Varg, why the extra question? You know how it works — everyone looks out for themselves or follows the money. I helped him with some of his special tasks before you showed up, and also after.
Understood. Alright, take care.
My respects. Finally, we meet. Situation is clearer now, but some complications arose.
What kind? Why didn’t you contact us sooner? What did you find out?
Didn’t contact sooner because there was no precise info on the task. I managed to find details about your missing group. Found traces. Several bodies. Most likely the whole group is dead…
Understood. We didn’t expect a happy ending, but hope, as you know, dies last. What about the scientists? Did you find traces of them?

I did, but here’s the tricky part. I know where they are hiding now, where they’ve been hiding from you, and that you still want them. I need guarantees that after I hand them over, nobody will put a bullet in my head…
Smart one, huh… Figured they were hiding from us. But maybe someone tipped you off? Anyway, not important now. Guarantees, you say — alright, what’s your proposal?

Old farms. No more than three of you. I come alone with the scientists. You hand over the promised money, we part ways. My sniper covers the site to keep anyone from breaking the deal. If that’s acceptable, let’s discuss the time…
Kid, aren’t you afraid? Setting conditions for Clear Sky? Do you even know what we could do to you?

When I got here — suddenly a Duty squad and a small Freedom unit blocked me. Heard of them, probably. See? I’m still alive, two recon squads are dead. After that, do you think I’m afraid to set conditions for some pitiful Southern Marsh clan that couldn’t even hire proper mercs?
Yeah, we underestimated you. We’ll fix that. Don’t get cocky, luck is fickle. Many want your head — don’t make us your enemies.
Better to be a living enemy than a dead pawn… So, you accept my terms?
One enemy more, one less. Doesn’t matter now. So, do you accept my terms?
What guarantees can you give? Why should we trust you? Where’s the proof that the scientists are still alive?
Only three scientists left. You wanted to kill them after they delivered something valuable to Strelok’s group. Enough proof for you?
Fine. We’ll be in touch…
You’ve been deceiving me from the start. I hope you don’t make that mistake this time. I’ll be waiting for our next meeting.
I’m here. What’s the rush? What’s so urgent?

Still asking? We found a Sinner agent. Remember I told you about them? Your so-called friend working for Freedom is actually a Sinner agent undercover. His real motives aren’t clear, but his plans regarding you are.
What plans?
Which plans exactly?
Sinners recruit more agents. You proved smart and capable — not just a shooter or artifact scavenger. He nearly recruited you.
Aren’t you doing the same thing?
No. I only want to protect you from him and his clan. Sinners are evil, a curse of the Zone. Treacherous soulless creatures that must be destroyed. I’ll do whatever it takes.
Alright. Suppose he’s a Sinner. What proof do you have?
Remember when you worked for Karim and Zmeelov? First time you faced an unknown anomaly with a psionic covering. Second time — Yakut the Hunter disappeared. All done by Anubis.
Psionic, huh. Probably the same one who attacked a scout from Khromy’s group.
Exactly. Finally, you’re understanding. We need to stop him at any cost.
But we don’t know what he wants…
I know what he wanted from you and how it ended. Most likely the Sinner’s interests match your current operations. You were supposed to meet Clear Sky agents regarding your task, right?
Supposed to. So?

Nothing good will come of it. I’ve learned more. We need to piece together how everything connects. Clear Sky tricked you — hired you to find a missing team and scientists. That team wasn’t for search — it was a strike team meant to kill those scientists. First attempt failed. Scientists fled here; I’ve tracked them.
Another twist. What a day… So they’re enemies too? Then who are you?

Politics. No friends here, only players working for certain people. Clear Sky lied from the start. Hired you and others to find the missing team and scientists — they wanted them dead because they crossed Clear Sky. First attempt failed, scientists fled here, I tracked them.
Wait. So I wasn’t supposed to save them, but lead Clear Sky to them so they could kill them? Damn… Why did these scientists anger Clear Sky so much?

They were to deliver secret equipment to a stalker named Strelok. He planned a trip to the Zone Center at Chernobyl and Pripyat; without it, he couldn’t pass the Burner. Clear Sky wants to block him. So they decided to eliminate anyone helping him. They attacked the scientists, but don’t know if the gear reached Strelok’s group. That’s why they needed you. After you do your part — they’d kill you as an extra witness.
Hm, even more shocking. Even learning Anubis is a Sinner agent didn’t surprise me that much. What’s in it for you?

Simple. Investment in the future. Pure business. Nobody can imagine what wealth the Zone Center holds — anomalies, mutants, artifacts. Millions. So I want Strelok’s mission to succeed. We need to figure out the Sinner’s goal and stop Clear Sky. I know what to do about the second, Anubis is trickier.
How do we handle it?

Here’s the plan: You deal with Clear Sky for now. I’ll find out the Sinner’s exact goal. Don’t contact him. Forget him. Go through Karim; he sets up your meeting with the Sinner agent. Tell them you found the scientists, but won’t hand them over for free. Trick them, make them accept your terms. Exchange for payment and guarantees. You might need to eliminate them. Then I’ll contact you with further instructions.
Alright. Hope I made the right choice.

The Clear Sky problem is sorted. You were right. They would’ve killed me if I’d just handed over the scientists’ location. By the way, I never found them, did I? Got any info on that? And did you learn anything about the Sinner’s plans?

I found the scientists. Even before meeting you. But I didn’t get the chance to lead them out. They told me the full story — how Clear Sky attacked them and why. Luckily, they managed to deliver the necessary equipment to Strelok’s people. After that, I went searching for Anubis, but when I returned, I only found bodies. He beat me to it.
That’s bad. So, what now?

Now we know for sure the Sinner agent is also on this. He cares about Strelok’s mission to the Zone center. What he hopes to gain is still unclear. Also, I only found three scientist bodies. There were six when I met them. Where the rest went, I don’t know. But that’s not the worst part.
What else happened?

Hasan. That bastard. The Syndicate bet on this freak in vain. Should’ve worked with Harold. Though probably the same outcome. Anyway — the locals found out you wiped out the Clear Sky squad. When you clashed with Duty and Freedom, they covered your ass. With the birds, it’s much worse. Probably the Sinner helped. They’ve put a bounty on you. So, base access is blocked.
Great. And now what do you want me to do this time?
Nothing. We’ll cover our tracks and head north. Hasan failed us. Time to end this game. Yes, we’ll lose a lot, but that’s the price for the mess we’ve made here.
So I have to eliminate all the stalkers at the base?

Yes. No choice. I’ll provide the gear you need. They’re under the Sinner’s influence. No other explanation. We take them out and leave. We’ll monitor Strelok’s mission. To hell with Anubis — nothing more for us here.
Understood.
Just so you know, I’ve got nothing to lose. I know what you’re capable of — make a move.
Relax. I won’t kill you — don’t even have a gun on me. Could’ve, many times — but didn’t…
Speak your piece, then we part ways.

Curious — what made you side with that fool Chekh? You know, there’s little difference between us Sinners and mercs. Everyone wants their slice of the Zone. Methods are almost the same — so why mercs?
Their goals are clearer to me. I’m a merc too, agreed to work for Clear Sky. But your aims remain a mystery. Care to enlighten me?
Why not. Simple. I need to open a path to the Zone center and block those who oppose it. In this case — Clear Sky. See? My goals align with mercs’…
Got it. Strelok’s mission is important to you, but why exactly?

You’ll find out soon, my friend. The mechanism’s in motion, unstoppable. Wonder where the other three scientists Chekh found went? I’ll tell you — they’re being led to the swamps, into the hands of those they tried to escape. But not by you, as planned — another merc. He’ll play a key role in the next act. Strelok starts his mission, the man follows. Many will die, the Zone will shake, but it’s the fair price… Everything resolves near Pripyat.
What are you even talking about? Which merc, damn it? Pripyat?
The real Pripyat. Anyway, I rambled. Hope you understood your role in this play. Part’s done, the second part’s the setup for act two. And remember — you’re like me. We’re alike. Thanks for that…
What?
I met Anubis. We talked, but now he’s dead. You won’t believe what happened…
What? How? Tell me.

I cleared the clan base of stalkers. Then the Sinner contacted me and suggested a meeting. I knew it was a trap but went to end it. I was wrong. He didn’t intend to kill me — we talked, then he shot himself…
Shot himself? Wait… What did you learn?

Just like that, he shot himself. Said his role is done, mine is just starting. Then — a bullet to the chin. He also told me what happened to the scientists he took from under your nose — sent them with some merc to the swamps. Why exactly — unclear. But our suspicion about Strelok’s group was right.
So that’s how it is. Clear enough then.
What exactly is clear?
Everything. We need to get out of here fast. Head north. I need to contact my command. Did he mention Pripyat?
Yeah, he mentioned it, but the meaning was lost.
You think that. In reality, it’s clear. Big shocks are coming to the Zone. And we’re meant to be at the center… Let’s go, I’ll explain on the way.
As you say…
